Mainstream Pop
Mainstream Pop is a broad, chart-focused style. It features instantly memorable
hooks, polished production, and direct emotional themes, blending dance, R&B,
electronic, rock, and hip-hop. The focus is always on catchy melodies, sing-along
choruses, and a clean, radio-ready sound.

Genre Tags:
Mainstream pop, contemporary pop, chart-pop, radio-pop, dance-pop, electro-pop,
synth-pop, EDM-pop, pop-rock, R&B-pop, indie-pop, commercial pop, modern pop
ballad, upbeat pop anthem, crossover pop

Mood Tags:
Catchy, upbeat, feel-good, romantic, playful, flirty, nostalgic, bittersweet, emotional,
cinematic, empowering, confident, dreamy, dramatic, uplifting, energetic, youthful,
inspirational, glamorous, polished

Instrument Tags:
Glossy synths, bright plucky synths, electronic drum kit, snap and clap grooves,
punchy kick and sub bass, electric bass, electric guitar, acoustic guitar strums, pop
piano, airy pads, rhythmic percussion loops, vocal chops, risers and FX, handclaps,
subtle orchestral strings, 808 bass, reversed swells

Vocal Tags:
Clean pop lead vocal, bright female vocal, smooth male vocal, mixed-voice belting,
powerful anthemic chorus, soft intimate verses, stacked harmonies, layered
background vocals, call-and-response phrases, polished pitch-corrected tone

Singer songwriter
Singer-Songwriter music centers on intimate storytelling, emotional clarity, and
organic instrumentation. It blends acoustic pop, folk-pop, and modern ballad styles
with a personal, confessional tone. Songs are built around voice, guitar or piano,
and honest lyrics, often highlighting vulnerability, reflection, and melodic simplicity.

Genre Tags:
Singer-songwriter, acoustic pop, indie-folk pop, folk-pop, acoustic ballad, piano
ballad, modern storyteller pop, intimate pop, contemporary acoustic

Mood Tags:
Emotional, reflective, tender, nostalgic, bittersweet, hopeful, sincere, romantic,
vulnerable, warm, melancholic, earnest, comforting, introspective, uplifting

Instrument Tags:
Acoustic guitar, fingerpicking guitar, strummed guitar, upright or warm electric
bass, soft piano, solo piano, light percussion, shaker and soft brush kit, gentle pads,
subtle strings, ambient textures, harmonica

Vocal Tags:
Soft intimate lead vocal, warm male vocal, airy female vocal, conversational
delivery, melodic storytelling, dynamic belting in chorus, layered harmonies, gentle
background vocals, breathy tone, raw emotional phrasing

Dance Pop
Dance Pop is an energetic genre that blends pop song structure with electronic
production. Songs usually feature steady four-on-the-floor rhythms, punchy
choruses, synths and pumping bass lines

Genre Tags:
Dance-pop, contemporary pop, club-pop, radio dance, EDM pop, electro-pop,
house-pop, festival-pop, commercial dance, modern dance

Mood Tags:
Upbeat, energetic, euphoric, feel-good, fun, flirty, confident, playful, uplifting,
empowering, anthemic, party-ready, infectious, bold, glamorous, carefree

Instrument Tags:
Four-on-the-floor kick, electronic drums, clap and snap, synth bass, bright synths,
plucky synths, lush pads, sidechained chords, risers and impacts, white-noise
sweeps, vocal chops, rhythmic FX,

Vocal Tags:
Strong pop lead vocal, bright female vocal, smooth male vocal, catchy topline
melodies, big belted choruses, repetitive hook phrases, stacked harmonies,
gang-style chant vocals, polished tuned tone, ad-lib fills around drops

Electro pop
Electro Pop is a synth-driven, hook-focused style that fuses pop songwriting with
electronic sound design. It features punchy programmed drums, bold basslines,
and melodic synth leads. The focus is on production,catchy motifs, and a modern,
futuristic edge.

Genre Tags:
Electro-pop, synth-pop, indie electro, electro-indie, electro dance-pop, alt
electro-pop, dark electro-pop, neon-pop, retro-future pop, indietronica, electronic
pop, left-field pop

Mood Tags:
Energetic, futuristic, dreamy, edgy, playful, icy, hypnotic, dramatic, bittersweet,
moody, pulsing, nostalgic, shimmering, confident, cool, stylish

Instrument Tags:
Analog synths, digital synths, arpeggiated synths, synth bass, sidechained pads,
electronic drum kit, drum machines, off beat hi-hats, glitchy FX, filtered noise
sweeps, vocoder layers, sampled textures, synthetic plucks, claps

Vocal Tags:
Clean pop lead vocal, airy female vocal, cool understated male vocal, robotic tone,
vocoder harmonies, autotuned, stacked chorus harmonies, chopped vocals,
backing lines, intimate verses with polished processed choruses

K-Pop
K-Pop is a performance-driven pop style from South Korea that blends hooks with
hip-hop, R&B, EDM, and dance. It features tight group vocals, complex
choreography, and bold visuals. Groups like Girls’ Generation, BIGBANG, BTS, and
BLACKPINK helped push it from local scene to global phenomenon.

Genre Tags:
K-pop, idol pop, boy-group pop, girl-group pop, dance-pop, hip-hop K-pop, R&B
K-pop, EDM-pop K-pop, teen-pop, concept pop

Mood Tags:
Catchy, energetic, dramatic, confident, playful, flirty, powerful, emotional, anthemic,
glamorous, intense, youthful,

Instrument Tags:
Electronic drum kit, punchy kicks and snares, 808 bass, synth brass, bright leads,
plucky synths, electric guitar, pop piano, trap hi-hats, claps, risers and FX, vocal
chops

Vocal Tags:
Tight group vocals, bright girl-group vocal, smooth boy-group vocal, mixed-voice
belting, rap verses, melodic rap, stacked harmonies, call-and-response hooks, chant
sections, ad-lib runs, polished tuned tone

Tip: DO NOT use the “-” when spelling “K-Pop”. Instead, spell it “Kpop” or "K Pop”, but
never “K-Pop”. When you use the hyphen Suno will ignore the genre completely

J-Pop
J-Pop is Japanese popular music that blends Western pop, rock, dance, and
electronic styles with idol culture, anime themes, and game music. It focuses on
sharp hooks, bright arrangements, and character-driven performances. From city
pop pioneers to modern idols and anime anthems, J-Pop ranges from cute and
bubbly to dramatic and emotional.

Genre Tags:
J-pop, idol pop, anime J-pop, anisong, city pop, electro J-pop, rock J-pop, shibuya-kei,
game-pop, teen-pop

Mood Tags:
Catchy, upbeat, cute, quirky, emotional, romantic, nostalgic, sparkly, dramatic,
hopeful, adventurous, youthful

Instrument Tags:
Electronic drum kit, bright snares, synth bass, chiptune leads, glossy pads, pop
piano, clean electric guitar, slap bass, string stabs, handclaps, synth arpeggios, FX
sweeps

Vocal Tags:
Clear high female vocal, smooth male tenor vocal, idol group unison, stacked
harmonies, call-and-response hooks, chant sections, rapid syllabic phrasing, big
chorus belting, Japanese-English mix, ad-lib runs, polished tuned tone

Y2K Pop
Y2K Pop is mainstream pop from the late ’90s and early 2000s. Artists like Britney
Spears, *NSYNC, Backstreet Boys, Christina Aguilera, Destiny’s Child, Spice Girls,
Jennifer Lopez, Mandy Moore, and Jessica Simpson helped define the sound with
catchy hooks, dance-pop beats and high-budget production. It blends teen pop,
R&B-pop, Eurodance, and pop-rock guitars with clap-heavy drums, and key-change
finales, capturing the era’s mix of mall culture, and arena-sized singalongs.

Genre Tags:
Y2K pop, teen pop, bubblegum pop, dance-pop, R&B-pop, pop-rock, Eurodance
pop, club-pop, radio-pop, boy-group pop, girl-group pop, pop ballad, slow-jam pop

Mood Tags:
Catchy, upbeat, fizzy, flirty, sassy, confident, playful, cheeky, romantic, lovestruck,
dramatic, angsty, bittersweet, empowering, determined, glamorous, sparkly, glossy,
bubbly, energetic, high-energy, danceable,

Instrument Tags:
Electronic drum kit, tight punchy kicks, bright snares, crisp claps, syncopated
hi-hats, synth bass, sub drops, sidechained pads, glossy synth leads, pop piano,
clean electric guitar stabs, acoustic guitar strums, string pads, simple synth bells,
vocoder accents, filtered intro EQ sweeps, risers and FX, vinyl-stop and
record-scratch accents

Vocal Tags:
Bright female pop vocal, smooth male tenor vocal, boy-group harmonies, girl-group
unison, stacked chorus layers, call-and-response hooks, talky pre-chorus lines,
whispered asides, melismatic ad-lib runs, big chorus belting, key-change final
chorus, “oh” and “yeah” backing ad-libs, doubled lead vocals, polished tuned tone

2010’s Pop
2010s Pop is global mainstream pop from roughly 2010–2019, where festival-sized
EDM, electro-pop, trap, and R&B all crash into the charts. Artists like Katy Perry,
Lady Gaga, Rihanna, Nicki Minaj, Trey Songz, Drake, Bruno Mars, Justin Bieber,
Ariana Grande, and Taylor Swift helped define the era with huge choruses, colourful
personas, and hook-heavy singles.

Genre Tags:
2010s pop, dance-pop, EDM-pop, electro-pop, synth-pop, trap-pop, R&B-pop,
club-pop, festival-pop, radio-pop, electropop ballad, trap ballad, tropical house pop,
hip-hop-infused pop, power-pop anthem.

Mood Tags:
Catchy, anthemic, euphoric, high-energy, confident, bold, empowering, fierce,
playful, flirty, cheeky, dramatic, intense, moody, seductive, glamorous, glossy,
party-ready, bittersweet, emotional, triumphant, rebellious,

Instrument Tags:
Electronic drum kit, punchy kicks, bright snares, crisp claps, trap hi-hats, deep 808
subs, synth bass, sidechained pads, supersaw synth leads, plucky synths, pop
piano, clean electric guitar stabs, atmospheric guitar swells, string pads, risers and
FX sweeps, EDM build-ups, vocal chops, filtered intro and drop

Vocal Tags:
Bright female pop vocal, agile melismatic runs, smooth male tenor vocal, airy
falsetto hooks, powerful belting choruses, rap verses, half-sung melodic rap,
call-and-response hooks, stacked chorus harmonies, gang shouts, chant sections,
processed backing vocals, subtly tuned leads, chopped vocal FX, ad-lib-heavy outros

Dark pop
Dark Pop is a moody, alternative branch of pop that blends indie, electronic, and
goth-influenced aesthetics with hooky, accessible songwriting. It leans on minor
keys and slow-to-mid tempos. Artists like Billie Eilish, Lorde, Lana Del Rey, Halsey,
Banks, Melanie Martinez, and The Weeknd helped push this sound into the
mainstream with introspective, emotionally charged songs about heartbreak,
mental health, and darker inner worlds.

Genre Tags:
Dark pop, alt-pop, electro dark-pop, indie pop, goth-pop, moody synth-pop,
cinematic pop, bedroom pop, art pop, trap-pop, downtempo pop, electro-noir pop.

Mood Tags:
Moody, brooding, melancholic, haunting, eerie, tense, hypnotic, nocturnal,
introspective, vulnerable, bittersweet, resigned, obsessive, dramatic, ominous,
unsettling, sensual, cathartic, fragile, cold, lonely, ethereal, ghostly, mysterious

Instrument Tags:
Electronic drum kit, dry punchy kicks, tight snares, sparse claps, trap hi-hats, deep
808 subs, gritty synth bass, detuned synth leads, ominous pads, reversed piano,
melancholic piano motifs, distant guitar swells, subtle distorted guitars, sub-bass
drones, granular FX.

Vocal Tags:
Whispery female vocal, airy head voice, smooth dark alto vocal, hushed male vocal,
close-mic intimate tone, layered whispers, stacked murmured harmonies,
breath-heavy delivery, half-spoken lines, melancholic melisma, haunting falsetto,
doubled lead vocals, vocoder shadows, pitch-shifted backing vocals, chopped vocal
FX, restrained but emotional choruses.

Pop ballad
Pop Ballad is emotionally driven pop that centers on intimate storytelling, and vocal
performance. Slow-to-mid tempo, it leans on piano or acoustic guitar, lush pads,
and dynamic builds that swell into huge choruses. Artists like Adele, Sam Smith,
Lewis Capaldi, Ed Sheeran, Celine Dion, and Whitney Houston have defined the
style with breakup anthems, power ballads, and heartfelt love songs.

Genre Tags:
Pop ballad, piano pop ballad, acoustic pop ballad, power ballad, soft-pop ballad,
R&B-pop ballad, modern pop ballad, cinematic pop ballad, adult contemporary pop,
radio pop ballad, duet pop ballad.

Mood Tags:
Emotional, heartfelt, vulnerable, romantic, longing, bittersweet, nostalgic, hopeful,
dramatic, introspective, tender, soul-stirring, cathartic, tearful, uplifting, yearning,
reflective, comforting, epic, sincere, soaring, intimate

Instrument Tags:
Acoustic piano, grand piano, soft electronic drum kit, gentle kick and snare, light
cymbals, warm electric bass, acoustic guitar strums, fingerpicked guitar, string
pads, full string section, subtle synth pads, ambient guitar swells, soft percussion,
reverse piano swells, risers and impacts

Vocal Tags:
Emotive female pop vocal, emotive male tenor vocal, breathy intimate verses,
powerful belted choruses, dynamic range, soulful melisma, long held notes, airy
falsetto, duet call-and-response, stacked chorus harmonies, echoing ad-libs,
key-change final chorus, close-mic storytelling tone, polished but expressive
delivery.

Acoustic pop
Acoustic Pop is hooky, radio-friendly pop built around mostly acoustic instruments
instead of heavy synths. Strums, fingerpicked patterns, and light organic percussion
carry catchy choruses and feel-good melodies. It sits between singer-songwriter
and mainstream pop: personal and intimate, but polished and structured

Genre Tags:
Acoustic pop, acoustic pop ballad, unplugged pop, acoustic dance-pop, coffeehouse
pop, indie acoustic pop, folk-pop, acoustic teen pop, acoustic radio pop, acoustic
R&B-pop, acoustic soft-pop.

Mood Tags:
Warm, gentle, breezy, uplifting, cozy, intimate, optimistic, romantic, nostalgic,
reflective, hopeful, easygoing, earnest, feel-good, heartfelt, mellow, sunny,
laid-back, comforting, sincere, relaxed, lighthearted

Instrument Tags:
Acoustic guitar strums, acoustic fingerpicking, light cajon groove, soft kick and
snare, handclaps, shaker, tambourine, warm electric bass, upright bass, pop piano,
subtle pad layers, sparse electric guitar fills, light string pads, vocal “ooh/ahh” beds,
minimal FX and reverb

Vocal Tags:
Clear pop vocal, soft male tenor vocal, bright female pop vocal, intimate close-mic
delivery, gentle head voice, light rasp, stacked acoustic harmonies,
call-and-response lines, singalong choruses, hummed outros, doubled lead vocals,
soft ad-libs, conversational phrasing, polished but natural tone

Bedroom Pop
Bedroom Pop is an internet-native, DIY strain of indie pop and alt-pop built on
intimate vocals, gentle melodies, and home-recorded production. Artists like Clairo,
beabadoobee, Cavetown, girl in red, boy pablo, Cuco, Gus Dapperton, Dayglow, and
Rex Orange County helped define the sound with lo-fi mixes, soft singing, and
deeply personal lyrics often made on laptops in bedrooms or tiny home studios

Genre Tags:
Bedroom pop, indie pop, lo-fi pop, lo-fi indie, dream pop, DIY pop, internet pop,
emo bedroom pop, indie rock-infused bedroom pop, folk-tinged bedroom pop,
alt-pop, chill pop.

Mood Tags:
Intimate, gentle, dreamy, hazy, nostalgic, bittersweet, shy, vulnerable, wistful, cozy,
mellow, laid-back, homespun, confessional, lonely, tender, warm, low-key,
introspective, sleepy-sweet, bedroomy, lo-fi, soft-focus

Instrument Tags:
Lo-fi drum loops, soft electronic drum kit, vinyl crackle, light percussion, warm bass,
chorus-soaked electric guitar, jangly clean guitars, bedroom-recorded acoustic
guitar, simple synth pads, vintage-style synths, toy keyboard tones, subtle ukulele,
ambient noise, tape hiss, DIY FX and subtle glitches.

Vocal Tags:
Soft indie vocal, breathy female vocal, gentle male tenor vocal, close-mic intimate
tone, slightly off-mic feel, doubled lead vocals, hushed harmonies, layered
“ooh/ahh” beds, imperfect but emotional takes, conversational phrasing, shy
delivery, subdued ad-libs, occasional falsetto flips.

Dream Pop
Dream Pop is an atmospheric branch of alternative and indie pop that puts mood,
texture, and reverb-soaked atmosphere on equal footing with melody. Guitars and
synths blur into hazy clouds while soft, often breathy vocals sit low in the mix, more
like another instrument than in front. Early pioneers like Cocteau Twins, Mazzy Star,
and The Sundays paved the way for modern acts such as Beach House, Cigarettes
After Sex, and Ethel Cain.

Genre Tags:
Dream pop, ethereal pop, indie dream pop, ambient pop, shoegaze-influenced pop,
slowcore-infused dream pop, cinematic dream pop, lo-fi dream pop, dreampop
ballad, atmospheric indie, alt-pop

Mood Tags:
Ethereal, hazy, nostalgic, bittersweet, dreamy, drifting, melancholic, tender,
cinematic, nocturnal, slow-burning, intimate, hypnotic, wistful, spectral, floating,
soft-focus, immersive, reflective, romantic, distant, velvety.

Instrument Tags:
Reverb-drenched electric guitar, chorus and tremolo guitar, jangly arpeggios,
delay-soaked leads, warm analog synth pads, soft drum kit, brushed or muffled
snares, simple kick patterns, deep but gentle bass, organ swells, subtle piano,
reverse FX, tape hiss, ambient noise beds, shimmer reverb tails

Vocal Tags:
Breathy female vocal, soft male tenor vocal, gauzy layered harmonies, distant
reverb-washed lead, intimate close-mic whispers, airy head voice, wordless
ooh/ahh textures, half-buried melodies, murmured lines, slow vibrato, duet-style
blend, stacked choral pads, emotionally restrained but haunting delivery.

Indie Pop
Indie Pop is hook-focused alternative pop that grew out of DIY, guitar-based scenes
in the late ’70s and ’80s UK, where bands released melodic, jangly pop on
independent labels instead of major-label pipelines. Early acts like Orange Juice and
other Postcard/Sarah Records bands helped define the sound’s bright guitars and
sensitive songwriting. The result is pop that’s melodic and approachable but still a
little off-center, handmade, and personal.

Genre Tags:
Indie pop, alt-pop, jangly indie pop, twee pop, bedroom indie pop, guitar-driven
indie pop, synthy indie pop, lo-fi indie pop, dream-pop-infused indie, indie
pop-rock, college indie pop, festival indie pop

Mood Tags:
Upbeat, bittersweet, nostalgic, quirky, romantic, introspective, earnest, breezy,
wistful, playful, DIY, warm, offbeat, soft-rebellious, charming, lighthearted,
melancholic, youthful, hopeful, laid-back, clever, personal.

Instrument Tags:
Clean electric guitars, jangly arpeggios, light overdrive guitars, acoustic guitar
strums, simple live drum kit, snare with light reverb, warm bass guitar, occasional
synth pads, analog-style lead synths, glockenspiel or bell accents, handclaps,
tambourine, subtle lo-fi textures, minimal FX

Vocal Tags:
Soft indie vocal, conversational male tenor vocal, clear but gentle female vocal,
slightly deadpan delivery, airy harmonies, group “ooh/ahh” backing, unpolished but
emotive tone, doubled lead vocals, call-and-response lines, singalong choruses,
subtle falsetto lifts, laid-back phrasing, intimate, diary-like lyrics.

Synth Pop
Synth Pop is pop music built around synthesizers, drum machines, and sequenced
basslines instead of guitar-driven bands. It emerged in the late ’70s and ’80s with
artists like Depeche Mode, New Order, Pet Shop Boys, Eurythmics, a-ha, and Yazoo,
then resurfaced through acts like CHVRCHES, La Roux, M83, and The 1975. It blends
sharp hooks with retro-futuristic textures, neon nostalgia, and cool, slightly
detached emotion.

Genre Tags:
Synth-pop, 80s synth-pop, modern synth-pop, electro-pop, new wave pop, retro
synth-pop, indie synth-pop, dark synth-pop, dance synth-pop, synth-pop ballad,
synthwave pop, cinematic synth-pop

Mood Tags:
Catchy, nostalgic, neon-lit, futuristic, sleek, moody, bittersweet, romantic, nocturnal,
dreamy, driving, dramatic, shimmering, atmospheric, cool, introspective, uplifting,
wistful, cinematic, hypnotic

Instrument Tags:
Analog synth bass, sequenced basslines, arpeggiated synths, bright synth leads,
poly synth pads, retro keys, electronic drum kit, drum machine grooves, gated
reverb snares, tight hi-hats, synth bells, chorus electric guitar, vocoder layers, FX
sweeps and risers

Vocal Tags:
Clear pop vocal, airy female vocal, plaintive male tenor vocal, cool detached
delivery, reverb-washed leads, doubled vocal lines, unison choruses,
call-and-response hooks, falsetto lifts, vocoder harmonies, stacked “ooh/ahh” pads,
melodic but slightly restrained tone

Avant-Pop
Avant-Pop is experimental, art-forward pop that pushes song structure, sound
design, and vocal performance into strange new shapes while still keeping some
kind of hook at the center. It blends art rock, electronic music, noise, and
hyper-detailed production with unusual melodies and surreal lyrics. Artists like
Björk, Kate Bush, Laurie Anderson, FKA twigs, SOPHIE, Charli XCX, Grimes, and Arca
helped shape the style, turning pop into a playground for bold, disruptive ideas.

Genre Tags:
Avant-pop, art pop, experimental pop, alt-pop, glitch-pop, deconstructed pop,
electronic avant-pop, hyper-detailed pop, leftfield pop, industrial pop, electro-art
pop, cinematic avant-pop

Mood Tags:
Surreal, off-kilter, edgy, strange, futuristic, unnerving, playful, provocative, intense,
cerebral, abstract, bold, alien, dreamy, twisted, dramatic, theatrical, fragmented,
unpredictable, eccentric, boundary-pushing

Instrument Tags:
Glitchy drums, chopped percussion, synthetic kicks and snares, granular FX, warped
synth bass, metallic synths, experimental sound design, prepared piano, noise
textures, bitcrushed leads, modular synths, reversed samples,

Vocal Tags:
Art-pop vocal, flexible female vocal, expressive male vocal, theatrical delivery,
whispered lines, yelps and gasps, processed and pitch-shifted vocals, vocoder
layers, chopped vocal samples, unconventional melodies, stacked experimental
harmonies, intimate to explosive dynamics, vocoder harmonies, stacked “ooh/ahh”
pads, melodic but slightly restrained tone

Hyper Pop
Hyperpop is a blown-out, internet-native strain of pop that turns chart tricks up to
absurd, chaotic extremes. It pushes glossy hooks through distortion, bitcrushed
drums, pitch-shifted vocals, and meme-brained sound design, drawing from EDM,
trap, pop-punk, and noise. Artists and collectives like PC Music, SOPHIE, A. G. Cook,
Charli XCX, and especially 100 gecs helped launch and popularize the style.

Genre Tags:
Hyperpop, glitch-pop, digicore, bubblegum bass, deconstructed pop, experimental
pop, electro hyperpop, trap-influenced hyperpop, punk-leaning hyperpop, club
hyperpop, hyperpop ballad, nightcore-adjacent pop

Mood Tags:
Maximalist, chaotic, hyperactive, noisy, sugary, playful, ironic, cathartic,
overstimulated, emotional, melodramatic, bittersweet, manic, glitchy, anxious,
rebellious, queer, surreal, internet-poisoned, meme-y, intense

Instrument Tags:
Drum machines, distorted 808s, rapid-fire hi-hats, clipped snares, blown-out kicks,
wobbling synth bass, brash supersaw leads, chiptune riffs, metallic plucks, harsh FX
swells, risers and sub drops, bitcrushed synths, granular glitches, sample chops,
nightcore-style pitch and tempo shifts, bass boosts

Vocal Tags:
Heavily autotuned vocal, pitched-up “chipmunk” vocal, formant-shifted leads,
layered shout hooks, bratty talk-singing, emo-influenced melodies, screamed
ad-libs, chopped-and-retriggered vocal FX, robotic vocoder doubles, rapid lyrical
repetition, intimate to explosive dynamics, vocoder harmonies,

Internet Pop
Internet Pop is digitally native pop built for timelines, Discord servers, and
algorithmic playlists more than radio. It blurs hyperpop, alt-pop, emo-rap, bedroom
pop, and EDM, leaning into meme culture, oversharing lyrics, and genre chaos.
Artists like Slayyyter, EDEN, Oliver Tree, PinkPantheress, glaive, underscores, and
Jane Remover are often framed as “internet pop” voices—breaking out via
SoundCloud, TikTok, and YouTube while sounding distinctly online-first

Genre Tags:
Internet pop, hyperpop-adjacent pop, alt internet pop, digicore pop, glitch-pop,
TikTok pop, meme-pop, bedroom internet pop, emo internet pop, trap-influenced
internet pop, cloud-pop, electro internet pop, DIY pop

Mood Tags:
Maximalist, chaotic, hyperactive, bittersweet, oversharing, playful, ironic, meme-y,
anxious, emotional, melodramatic, nostalgic, terminally-online, self-aware, surreal,
cathartic, rebellious, glitchy, vulnerable, zoomed-in, impulsive

Instrument Tags:
Drum machines, distorted 808s, rapid hi-hats, clipped snares, blown-out kicks,
wobbling synth bass, bright supersaw leads, chiptune riffs, plucky digital bells, airy
cloud pads, lo-fi electric guitar, sample chops, phone notification FX, granular
glitches, risers and sub drops

Vocal Tags:
Heavily autotuned vocal, pitched-up “chipmunk” vocal, emo-rap sing-song, bratty
talk-singing, soft bedroom whisper vocal, layered shout hooks, call-and-response
chants, stacked harmonies, vocoder doubles, ad-lib spam, chopped and retriggered
vocal FX, hyper-emotional but casually delivered lines.

City Pop
City Pop is a sleek, urban strain of Japanese pop from the late ’70s and ’80s that
blends soft rock, AOR, funk, disco, jazz fusion, and R&B with glossy studio
production. It soundtracks car stereos, night drives, and seaside highways during
Japan’s economic boom. Artists like Tatsuro Yamashita, Mariya Takeuchi, Miki
Matsubara, Anri, Taeko Ohnuki, and Junko Yagami defined the style with smooth
grooves, sophisticated chords, and bittersweet, adult romance

Genre Tags:
City pop, 80s city pop, Japanese city pop, AOR city pop, soft-rock city pop, funk city
pop, disco city pop, boogie city pop, yacht city pop, jazz-fusion city pop, summer city
pop, night-drive city pop, coastal city pop, city pop ballad

Mood Tags:
Smooth, breezy, sophisticated, summery, laid-back, groovy, romantic, nostalgic,
bittersweet, polished, classy, relaxed, optimistic, urbane, midnight-cruise, carefree,
mellow, flirtatious, reflective, silky, feel-good, lightly melancholic

Instrument Tags:
Tight studio drum kit, punchy soft kicks, crisp snares, funk bass, slap bass, clean
chorus electric guitar, muted rhythm chops, jazzy extended chords, Rhodes electric
piano, bright pop piano, analog synth pads, synth brass stabs, string pads,
saxophone solos, horn section riffs, congas and shakers, subtle percussion fills

Vocal Tags:
Smooth male tenor vocal, airy female pop vocal, light falsetto lifts, gentle vibrato,
relaxed but precise phrasing, Japanese lead vocal, Japanese–English phrase mix,
stacked chorus harmonies, soft call-and-response lines, unison hooks, ad-libbed
runs at outro, polished but warm tone, intimate yet radio-ready delivery.

Latin Pop
Latin Pop is pop rooted in Latin America, Spain, and the U.S. Latin scene, blending
global pop hooks with regional rhythms like reggaetón, dembow, salsa, bachata,
and tropical. From ’90s and 2000s superstars like Ricky Martin, Shakira, Jennifer
Lopez, Enrique Iglesias, Luis Fonsi, and Gloria Estefan to modern urbano and
reggaetón-driven acts like J Balvin, Bad Bunny, Rosalía, Karol G, and Camila Cabello,
Latin Pop has taken its mix of dancefloor grooves and romantic melodrama.

Genre Tags:
Latin pop, Latin dance-pop, urbano Latin pop, reggaeton-pop, tropical pop,
salsa-pop, bachata-pop, Latin pop ballad, electro Latin pop, trap-Latin pop, radio
Latin pop, acoustic Latin pop

Mood Tags:
Catchy, rhythmic, romantic, sensual, flirty, passionate, celebratory, upbeat, festive,
dramatic, emotional, longing, nostalgic, hopeful, confident, empowering, playful,
heartfelt, summery, nightlife-ready, infectious

Instrument Tags:
Reggaetón dembow drum pattern, punchy electronic kicks, 808 subs, congas,
timbales, bongos, shakers, syncopated rhythm guitar, nylon acoustic guitar lines,
bright synth leads, lush pads, piano montunos, brass and horn riffs, string swells,
crowd chants.

Vocal Tags:
Warm Spanish-language lead vocal, powerful female belting, smooth male tenor
vocal, light falsetto lifts, expressive melisma, emotional ad-lib runs,, stacked chorus
harmonies, group shouts, singalong hooks, Spanish–English (Spanglish) mix,
rhythmic phrasing, conversational verses with big soaring choruses

EuroPop
Europop is a sleek, hook-heavy style of European pop that grew out of late-’70s
disco, AOR, and synth-pop, built for radio, Eurovision stages, and holiday clubs. It
leans on simple, instantly memorable choruses, danceable beats, and glossy studio
polish. ABBA helped define the template, followed by acts like Modern Talking,
Roxette, Ace of Base, Aqua, Vengaboys, Eiffel 65, and other pan-European hits that
blurred into Eurodance and club pop across the ’80s and ’90s

Genre Tags:
Europop, Euro-pop, Eurodisco pop, Eurodance-pop, Italo-disco pop, Scandinavian
pop, Euro club-pop, Euro teen-pop, bubblegum Euro-pop, dance-pop, Euro
radio-pop, Eurovision-style pop

Mood Tags:
Catchy, upbeat, carefree, glossy, playful, romantic, feel-good, escapist, anthemic,
lighthearted, nostalgic, danceable, sugary, optimistic, cheesy-in-a-good-way,
party-ready, fun, effervescent, simple, singalong

Instrument Tags:
Electronic drum kit, punchy four-on-the-floor or disco-influenced beats, bright
snares and claps, synth bass, octave bass lines, shimmering polysynth chords,
simple piano hooks, Euro-style synth leads, occasional guitar chanks, handclaps,
tambourine, string pads, synth brass, FX risers and filtered sweep intros.

Vocal Tags:
Clear pop vocal, bright female lead vocal, smooth male tenor vocal, big unison
choruses, stacked harmonies, shout-along refrains, call-and-response hooks, simple
English or mixed-language lyrics, memorable title-line repeat, occasional spoken or
rap-style middle sections, polished but straightforward delivery

Classic Rock
Classic Rock is guitar-driven rock from the late ’60s through the ’80s, rooted in
blues, hard rock, and arena-sized songwriting. Bands like The Rolling Stones, Led
Zeppelin, The Who, Queen, Pink Floyd, AC/DC, Aerosmith, Fleetwood Mac, and Tom
Petty & The Heartbreakers helped define it with big riffs, memorable choruses,
extended solos, and larger-than-life performances. It stretches from bluesy swagger
and psychedelic jams to power ballads and fist-pumping anthems that still
dominate rock radio and festival stages.

Genre Tags:
Classic rock, blues rock, hard rock, arena rock, stadium rock, boogie rock, southern
rock, folk rock, psychedelic rock, 70s rock, 80s rock, rock ballad, power ballad,
riff-rock, guitar-hero rock

Mood Tags:
Energetic, powerful, rebellious, gritty, bold, anthemic, driving, confident, nostalgic,
triumphant, rowdy, soulful, emotional, dramatic, laid-back, road-trip-ready, bluesy,
cathartic, stadium,

Instrument Tags:
Live drum kit, open hi-hats, tom fills, overdriven electric guitars, big rock riffs, power
chords, bluesy lead guitar, guitar solos, bass guitar grooves, rock organ
Hammond-style, piano, analog synth accents, tambourine, handclaps, crowd
shouts, natural amp and room reverb

Vocal Tags:
Classic rock vocal, raspy male rock vocal, powerful belting, high rock tenor, soulful
blues-influenced delivery, gang shouts, stacked harmony vocals, big hooks, female
rock vocal, gritty ad-libs, sustained high notes,

Radio Rock
Radio Rock is mainstream, hook-driven rock built for FM and streaming playlists
from the late ’90s onward. It blends alt-rock, post-grunge, pop-punk, and arena rock
into big choruses, polished production, and mid-to-up-tempo songs that sit
comfortably between pop and hard rock. Bands like Foo Fighters, Nickelback, The
Killers, Kings of Leon, and Imagine Dragons helped define the sound with riffy
verses, explosive choruses

Genre Tags:
Radio rock, modern rock, alt rock, pop-rock, post-grunge, arena rock, hard rock,
mainstream rock, pop-punk-influenced rock, emo-influenced rock, stadium rock,
radio-ready rock ballad

Mood Tags:
Anthemic, energetic, driving, bold, confident, emotional, cathartic, uplifting,
powerful, catchy, nostalgic, bittersweet, angsty, determined, rebellious,
radio-friendly, triumphant, punchy, motivational, singalong,

Instrument Tags:
Live rock drum kit, punchy kick and snare, crisp cymbals, tom fills, overdriven
rhythm guitars, tight palm-muted verses, big open chorus chords, melodic lead
guitar lines, bass guitar locked to the drums, thick layered guitar stacks, occasional
synth pads or textures, piano for ballads, claps and crowd-style hits,

Vocal Tags:
Rock vocal, strong male tenor vocal, bright female rock vocal, gritty edge on high
notes, powerful belting choruses, emotional midrange, stacked chorus harmonies,
doubled lead vocals, gang “whoa/hey” shouts, call-and-response hooks, chant
sections, conversational verse phrasing

Hard Rock
Hard Rock is loud, riff-first rock built on distorted guitars, big drums, and
high-impact vocals. Born from late-’60s blues rock and heavy rock, it favors
power-chords, guitar-solos, and arena-ready hooks. Bands like Led Zeppelin, Deep
Purple, AC/DC, Aerosmith, Van Halen, Def Leppard, and Guns N’ Roses define it.

Genre Tags:
Hard rock, heavy rock, classic hard rock, 70s hard rock, 80s hard rock, arena hard
rock, riff rock, guitar-hero rock, blues rock, bluesy hard rock, boogie hard rock,
glam-leaning hard rock, radio-ready hard rock, hard rock anthem, hard rock ballad

Mood Tags:
Aggressive, driving, gritty, swaggering, bold, rebellious, defiant, intense,
high-energy, rowdy, anthemic, triumphant, punchy, tough, fearless, confrontational,
cathartic, dramatic, urgent, reckless, sexy, stomping, hard-edged, electrifying,
larger-than-life

Instrument Tags:
Distorted electric guitars, overdrive/fuzz, power chords, palm-muted chugs, riff-led
verses, open-chord chorus walls, lead guitar hooks, extended guitar solos, feedback
squeals, wah pedal, live drum kit, big kick and snare crack, crash-heavy cymbals,
tom fills, bass guitar locked to drums, occasional Hammond/rock organ

Vocal Tags:
Raspy male rock vocal, gritty edge, high rock tenor, belted choruses, sustained high
notes, bluesy phrasing, shout accents, call-and-response hooks, gang vocals,
stacked harmonies, ad-lib screams/wails, big singalong refrains, dynamic verses
exploding into huge choruses

Prompt #1151

chords, crash-heavy drums, bass locked to kick, wah lead fills, male gritty tenor vocal, gang
shouts, song about kicking open the door to your old life and not asking permission

Alternative Rock
Alternative Rock is rock that grew from the ’70s–’80s underground and college radio
as a counterpoint to mainstream rock. It spans jangle, post-punk, noise, shoegaze,
grunge, and Britpop, usually guitar-driven with an indie/DIY edge. Acts like R.E.M.,
The Cure, Pixies, Nirvana, Radiohead, and Oasis helped define it.

Genre Tags:
Alternative rock, alt-rock, college rock, modern rock, indie rock, post-punk, jangle
pop, noise rock, noise pop, grunge, Britpop, shoegaze, art rock, pop-rock alt, radio
alternative

Mood Tags:
Angsty, rebellious, restless, urgent, introspective, bittersweet, moody, raw, punchy,
driving, defiant, sarcastic, yearning, melancholic, optimistic, dramatic, uneasy,
romantic, vulnerable, explosive, crunchy, hooky

Instrument Tags:
Live drum kit, punchy kick and snare, tight hi-hats, tom builds, overdriven rhythm
guitars, clean chorus guitar arpeggios, fuzz/distortion leads, pedal textures
(delay/chorus/phaser), melodic basslines, occasional acoustic-less clean electrics,
synth pads (optional), feedback swells, riff-to-hook dynamic shifts, roomy reverb

Vocal Tags:
Conversational verse delivery, gritty male rock vocal, bright female alt vocal,
nasal/edgy tone, breathy intimate lines, belted choruses, shouted accents, stacked
chorus harmonies, call-and-response hooks, ad-lib yells, dynamic soft-to-loud
phrasing

Indie Rock
Indie Rock is guitar-driven rock tied to independent labels and DIY scenes,
emerging from 1980s college/underground rock. It ranges from jangly pop and
post-punk to noisy lo-fi and art-leaning rock, usually prioritizing personality over
polish. Artists like R.E.M., The Smiths, Pixies, Sonic Youth, Pavement, Arcade Fire,
and The Strokes shaped the sound

Genre Tags:
Indie rock, alternative/indie rock, college rock, lo-fi indie rock, noise pop, jangle
rock, post-punk indie, slacker rock, art rock, garage rock revival, indie anthem, indie
rock ballad, indie dance-rock

Mood Tags:
Raw, punchy, restless, bittersweet, earnest, anxious, romantic, introspective, ironic,
defiant, moody, warm, hopeful, wistful, messy, intimate, melodic, jangly, quirky,
cathartic, brooding, euphoric, DIY, off-kilter

Instrument Tags:
Live drum kit, tight kick and snare, driving hi-hats, crunchy rhythm guitars, clean
arpeggiated guitars, chorus/delay pedals, fuzz leads, feedback swells, melodic
basslines, simple synth pads (optional), tambourine, handclaps, room reverb

Vocal Tags:
Conversational verses, deadpan delivery, nasal edge, breathy intimate tone, belted
chorus lift, shouted accents, stacked harmonies, doubled leads, call-and-response
hooks, gang “oh” backing, ad-lib yelps, dynamic soft-to-loud phrasing

guitars, fuzz lead hook, melodic bassline, handclap accents, male vocal, shout-lift chorus,
song about stealing back your confidence on a midnight city walk

Pop Rock
Pop Rock is hook-heavy rock that fuses pop melodies and songwriting with
guitar-band energy and a radio-ready, polished sound. It spans power pop and soft
rock to modern chart pop-rock. Acts like The Beatles, The Beach Boys, Fleetwood
Mac, Bon Jovi, Maroon 5, and OneRepublic are common touchstones.

Genre Tags:
Pop rock, pop-rock, pop/rock, guitar pop, radio pop-rock, power pop, soft rock,
adult contemporary pop-rock, arena pop-rock, jangle pop, pop-punk-influenced
pop rock, synth-pop rock, rock-pop crossover, modern pop rock

Mood Tags:
Catchy, upbeat, bright, upbeat-romantic, feel-good, energetic, bouncy, confident,
playful, flirty, hopeful, uplifting, anthemic, singalong-ready, glossy, breezy,
bittersweet, nostalgic, heartfelt, determined, dramatic, warm, punchy

Instrument Tags:
Live drum kit, punchy kick and snare, crisp hi-hats, claps, tambourine, clean electric
guitar arpeggios, crunchy chorus power chords, palm-muted verse riffs, melodic
lead guitar hooks, bass guitar groove, pop piano, synth pads, synth arpeggios, risers
and FX sweeps

Vocal Tags:
Clean pop-rock vocal, smooth male tenor vocal, bright female pop vocal,
conversational verses, big belted choruses, doubled lead vocals, stacked chorus
harmonies, call-and-response hooks, gang “oh-oh” sections, tight backing vocals,
ad-lib runs, polished radio-ready tone

Pop Punk
Pop Punk is fast, hooky punk-rock with power-pop melodies, bright choruses, and
loud guitars. Popularized in the ’90s–2000s by Green Day, Weezer, Blink-182, The
Offspring, Sum 41, New Found Glory, Paramore, and Fall Out Boy, it turns angst and
humor into singalong anthems

Genre Tags:
Pop punk, pop-punk, punk-pop, skate punk (pop-punk leaning), 90s pop punk,
2000s pop punk, emo pop, radio pop punk, power pop punk, neon pop punk,
modern pop punk, pop punk ballad

Mood Tags:
Catchy, energetic, punchy, youthful, rebellious, cheeky, sarcastic, angsty, cathartic,
confident, reckless, playful, flirty, romantic, bittersweet, nostalgic, impulsive,
restless, defensive, dramatic, triumphant, rowdy, heartfelt, determined,
self-deprecating, singalong-ready

Instrument Tags:
Fast live drum kit, snappy snare, driving hi-hats, tom fills, palm-muted verse guitars,
distorted power-chord choruses, octave lead riffs, bright single-note hooks, bass
guitar locked to kick, quick stops and dropouts, occasional clean breakdown
guitars, handclaps, crowd stomps, gang-shout accents

Vocal Tags:
Nasal male pop-punk vocal, bright female pop-punk vocal, talk-sung verses, urgent
phrasing, shouted accents, big belted choruses, stacked harmonies, gang vocals,
call-and-response hooks, “whoa/oh” singalong sections, ad-lib yells, key-change or
final-chorus lift

beach-day victory lap

Punk Rock
Punk Rock is fast, stripped-down guitar rock born from mid-1970s DIY scenes. It
favors short, loud songs, simple riffs, and anti-establishment attitude over studio
polish. Bands like Ramones, Sex Pistols, The Clash, Buzzcocks, Dead Kennedys,
Black Flag, and Bad Brains shaped the sound and its hardcore offshoots

Genre Tags:
Punk rock, 77 punk, garage punk, street punk, hardcore punk, melodic hardcore,
anarcho-punk, Oi!, skate punk, art punk, d-beat, post-punk, punk revival

Mood Tags:
Aggressive, raw, rebellious, defiant, confrontational, anti-establishment, sarcastic,
sneering, urgent, frenetic, high-energy, gritty, chaotic, cathartic, angry, streetwise,
political, nihilistic, reckless, DIY, noisy, relentless, unfiltered, shout-along

Instrument Tags:
Fast live drum kit, snappy snare crack, straight eighth-note hi-hats, crash-heavy
chorus cymbals, quick tom fills, distorted rhythm guitar, downstroked power
chords, simple riff hooks, minimal/short solos, buzzing amp grit, feedback squeals,
picked bass eighth-notes, tight bass-to-kick lock, lo-fi room edge, quick stop-start
breaks

Vocal Tags:
Sneering punk vocal, shouted hooks, nasal male punk vocal, raw female punk vocal,
talk-sung verses, barked phrases, gang vocals, call-and-response chants, gang “oh”
refrains, unpolished takes, doubled lead vocal, ad-lib yelps/screams, fast syllabic
delivery

Emo Rock
Emo Rock is emotional, melody-forward rock from punk’s “emocore” roots, built on
confessional lyrics, dynamic soft-to-loud arrangements, and ringing guitars that
explode into distorted choruses. From Rites of Spring and Sunny Day Real Estate to
Jimmy Eat World, My Chemical Romance, and Paramore, it turns personal turmoil
into big hooks

Genre Tags:
Emo rock, emo, emocore, emotional hardcore, Midwest emo, indie emo, emo-pop,
post-hardcore emo, screamo, emo revival, alternative emo, pop-punk emo

Mood Tags:
Angsty, cathartic, confessional, vulnerable, intense, bittersweet, nostalgic, dramatic,
yearning, restless, melancholic, heartfelt, anxious, romantic, moody, explosive, raw,
tender, lonely, desperate, hopeful, resentful, self-critical, defiant, pleading,
reflective

Instrument Tags:
Live drum kit, punchy snare, driving hi-hats, dynamic quiet-to-loud builds, clean
arpeggiated guitars, chorus/delay pedal shimmer, palm-muted verse riffs, distorted
power-chord choruses, octave lead lines, feedback swells, melodic basslines,
occasional piano or clean breakdown sections

Vocal Tags:
Emotive male tenor vocal, bright female emo vocal, close-mic intimate verses,
strained high notes, cracked delivery, spoken/confessional lines, shouted accents,
big belted choruses, stacked harmonies, gang vocals, call-and-response hooks,
occasional screamed backing lines

Post Punk
Post Punk is the late-’70s wave that kept punk’s DIY urgency but pushed into artier
territory. Pulling from art rock, disco, dub/reggae, and krautrock, with bass-forward
grooves, angular guitars, early synth textures. Touchstones include Public Image
Ltd, Joy Division, Wire, Gang of Four, Talking Heads, Siouxsie and the Banshees.

Genre Tags:
Post Punk, post-punk, postpunk, early post-punk, art punk, experimental punk, UK
post-punk, dance-punk, dub-influenced post-punk, angular rock, no wave,
coldwave, minimal wave, gothic post-punk, post-punk revival, avant-punk

Mood Tags:
Tense, anxious, alienated, detached, icy, nocturnal, bleak, brooding, paranoid,
restless, urgent, confrontational, political, introspective, sardonic, ironic,
claustrophobic, dystopian, mechanical, hypnotic, edgy, nervous, simmering,
sinister, romantic-dark, cynical, jittery, ominous, angular, minimalist

Instrument Tags:
Bass-forward grooves, melodic picked bass, chorus/flanger bass, angular clean
guitar, staccato riffs, scratchy funk guitar, chorus/delay guitar shimmer, spring
reverb hits, tight live drum kit, dry punchy snare, motorik beat, tom-driven patterns,
cowbell/percussion accents, analog synth leads, synth pads, sequenced synth lines,
occasional drum machine, tape-echo feedback swells

Vocal Tags:
Deadpan baritone vocal, detached monotone delivery, urgent talk-sung verses,
sneering punk edge, clipped phrasing, chant vocals, shout accents,
call-and-response hooks, gang chants, icy female vocal, anxious male tenor, gothic
croon, spoken-word sections, minimal harmonies, reverb-drenched vocal lines

Grunge
Grunge is the late-’80s/early-’90s Seattle “sound”: murky, sludgy distorted guitars
and loud-soft dynamics that fuse punk attitude with heavy-metal weight, paired
with angst-filled, introspective lyrics. Core bands include Nirvana, Pearl Jam,
Soundgarden, Alice in Chains, Mudhoney, and Screaming Trees.

Genre Tags:
Grunge, grunge rock, Seattle sound, Seattle grunge, alternative rock, punk-metal
hybrid, sludge-tinged rock, garage-influenced grunge, hard rock grunge, alt-metal
crossover, ’90s alternative, post-grunge, Pacific Northwest rock

Mood Tags:
Angsty, bleak, apathetic, cynical, raw, gritty, bitter, moody, brooding, heavy, weary,
resentful, frustrated, self-loathing, sardonic, nihilistic, tense, anxious, wounded,
defiant, melancholic, aggressive, introspective, exhausted, ominous, smoky, grimy

Instrument Tags:
Fuzz/overdrive electric guitars, thick distorted rhythm guitar, power-chord riffs,
drop-D tuning, sludgy mid-tempo chugs, loud-soft verse/chorus dynamics, feedback
squeals, ringing open chords, gritty lead guitar bends, heavy bass guitar, pick-style
bass attack, live drum kit, punchy snare, big kick drum, crash-heavy choruses, tom
fills, minimal studio polish, room-mic grit

Vocal Tags:
Raspy male vocal, gritty baritone, strained tenor peaks, half-sung half-spoken
delivery, lethargic drawl verses, shouted chorus accents, throatier scream bursts,
imperfect pitch for realism, close-mic confessional takes, double-tracked choruses,
sparse harmonies, antagonistic sneer, weary melancholic tone, raw unpolished
vocal sound

Ska
Ska is Jamaica’s late-’50s/early-’60s dance music: fast tempos, a walking bass line,
and sharp offbeat “skank” guitar/keys, often with jazzy horn lines. Blending
mento/calypso with American R&B and jazz, it set the blueprint for rocksteady and
reggae.

Genre Tags:
Ska, Jamaican ska, traditional ska, first-wave ska, 1960s ska, classic ska, blue beat,
rude boy era, Kingston sound, sound system ska, ska jazz, horn-driven ska,
dancehall-precursor ska, rocksteady precursor, Jamaican R&B

Mood Tags:
Upbeat, joyful, bouncy, danceable, sunny, playful, cheeky, celebratory, flirtatious,
energetic, lively, buoyant, swinging, groovy, cool, carefree, optimistic, feel-good,
communal, party-ready, vintage, beachy, spirited, infectious, bright, soulful,
swaggering, rebellious, lighthearted, punchy, crisp, skippy

Instrument Tags:
Offbeat guitar, offbeat piano chops, Hammond organ, walking bassline, upright
bass, electric bass guitar, trumpet stabs, trombone lines, saxophone leads, drum
kit, rimshot snare, shuffle groove, ride-cymbal swing, hi-hat upbeats, congas,
bongos, shakers, timbales, handclaps, percussion breaks, tape echo, spring reverb

Vocal Tags:
Jamaican patois vocal, upbeat male tenor, warm male baritone, soulful female
vocal, call-and-response vocals, group harmonies, unison chant hook, toasting/MC
chatter, talk-sung lines, playful ad-libs, shouted interjections, singalong chorus,
reverb-drenched vocal, vintage mono vocal tone sparse harmonies, antagonistic
sneer, weary melancholic tone, raw unpolished vocal sound

Ska Punk
Ska Punk fuses ska’s offbeat skank and walking bass with punk’s speed, volume,
and DIY bite—often adding bright horn lines over double-time drums. Emerging
from late-’70s UK cross-pollination and later tied to ’90s third-wave scenes, it’s built
for both dancing and moshing (Operation Ivy, The Mighty Mighty Bosstones, Less
Than Jake)

Genre Tags:
Ska Punk, ska-punk, ska punk, skacore, ska-core, horn punk, punk ska, ska revival
punk, third-wave ska punk, 2 Tone-influenced ska punk, melodic ska punk,
hardcore ska, skate ska, reggae-punk fusion, East Bay ska punk

Mood Tags:
Upbeat, rowdy, rebellious, high-energy, sweaty, playful, punchy, bouncy, chaotic,
reckless, defiant, sarcastic, mischievous, party-ready, anthemic, shouty, cathartic,
riotous, urgent, bratty-fun, cheeky, streetwise, confrontational, sunny, fast, skippy,
celebratory, “pick-it-up” hype, restless, loud

Instrument Tags:
Offbeat guitar skank, distorted punk rhythm guitar, palm-muted verse chugs, fast
power-chords, walking bassline, picked electric bass attack, double-time drum kit,
snare crack backbeat, fast hi-hat drive, crash-heavy choruses, stop-time breaks,
horn section, unison horn hooks, organ stabs, short guitar upstroke breakdowns

Vocal Tags:
Jamaican patois vocal, upbeat male tenor, warm male baritone, soulful female
vocal, group harmonies, unison chant hook, toasting/MC chatter, talk-sung lines,
playful ad-libs, shouted interjections, reverb-drenched vocal, vintage mono vocal,
raw unpolished vocal sound

Rockabilly
Rockabilly is an early rock-and-roll style from the mid-1950s American South,
blending country “hillbilly” drive with R&B rhythms. Defined by slap-back echo,
twangy lead guitar, and upright bass “slap,” it was popularized on early Sun
recordings by Elvis Presley, Carl Perkins, and Jerry Lee Lewis

Genre Tags:
Rockabilly, rockabilly rock and roll, 1950s rockabilly, classic rockabilly, Sun Records
sound, hillbilly bop, rock and roll roots, rockabilly revival, neo-rockabilly,
psychobilly, roots rockabilly, country boogie, rockabilly shuffle

Mood Tags:
Rebellious, swaggering, rowdy, upbeat, lively, punchy, wild, flirtatious, playful,
cheeky, hot-blooded, confident, gritty, raw, restless, high-energy, dancefloor-ready,
mischievous, retro-cool, joyful, loose, infectious, foot-stomping, fast-talking, cocky,
reckless, bright, wiry, driving, celebratory

Instrument Tags:
Hollow-body electric guitar, twangy lead licks, fingerpicked guitar, palm-muted
chugs, slapback delay/echo, tape echo, spring reverb, upright bass slap, walking
bassline, boogie-woogie piano, minimal drum kit, train beat groove, snare rimshots,
ride-cymbal swing, handclaps, tambourine accents, short guitar solos, stop-time
hits

Vocal Tags:
Male rock-and-roll tenor, hiccup vocal delivery, gritty baritone, yodel-like inflections,
shouty chorus accents, talk-sung verses, playful ad-libs, reverb-drenched vocal,
slap-echo vocal, tight duet harmonies, doo-wop style backing vocals, confident
crooner tone, female rockabilly vocal, call-and-response hooks.

Celtic Punk
Celtic Punk fuses punk rock’s speed and shout-along grit with Irish/Scottish folk
melody—distorted guitars and double-time drums alongside fiddle, tin whistle,
accordion, and sometimes bagpipes. Popularized in the 1980s by The Pogues, later
carried by Dropkick Murphys and Flogging Molly.

Genre Tags:
Celtic punk, Irish punk, Scottish punk, folk punk, Celtic folk punk, Celtic rock punk,
pub punk, punk folk fusion, Oi!-tinged Celtic punk, working-class punk, bagpipes
punk, fiddle-led punk, Boston Celtic punk, diaspora punk, punk shanty

Mood Tags:
Rowdy, rousing, rebellious, defiant, proud, communal, anthemic, shouty, punchy,
high-energy, reckless, celebratory, boisterous, spirited, triumphant, gritty,
confrontational, streetwise, nostalgic, homesick, bittersweet, sentimental, heartfelt,
mournful, fiery, urgent, mischievous, hard-luck, pub-singalong, riotous, cathartic

Instrument Tags:
Offbeat punk guitar, distorted power-chord rhythm guitar, fast downstrokes,
double-time drum kit, snare crack backbeat, crash-heavy choruses, driving bass
guitar, walking folk-style bass runs, fiddle, tin whistle, accordion, mandolin, banjo,
bouzouki, uilleann pipes, bagpipes, bodhrán, hand percussion, unison folk hooks,
call-and-response gang shouts, stomp-clap breaks

Vocal Tags:
Raspy male punk vocal, gritty baritone, nasal tenor edge, shout-sung verses,
melodic singalong chorus, gang vocals, call-and-response hooks, pub-chant
refrains, spoken interjections, toasting/MC chatter, Irish/Scottish-accented delivery,
harmony stacks on chorus, shouted ad-libs, crowd-chant endings

Hair Metal
Hair Metal is late-’80s, MTV-era glam metal: Hook heavy hard rock with big
choruses, guitar solos, and power ballads, paired with teased hair and makeup.
Born from the L.A. scene and built for radio and arenas, it peaked before grunge
flipped tastes in the early ’90s (Mötley Crüe, Poison, Def Leppard, Bon Jovi).

Genre Tags:
Hair Metal, hair rock, glam metal, pop metal, melodic hard rock, arena metal,
Sunset Strip metal, L.A. metal, ’80s hard rock, sleaze metal, glam hard rock,
commercial metal, power ballad rock, MTV metal, radio metal

Mood Tags:
Anthemic, cocky, glossy, flashy, high-energy, party-ready, hedonistic, rebellious,
flirtatious, lusty, romantic, triumphant, feel-good, larger-than-life, dramatic,
over-the-top, celebratory, rowdy, confident, punchy, adrenaline, slick, hooky,
sentimental, heartbroken, defiant, playful, cheeky, bold, stadium-ready, theatrical

Instrument Tags:
High-gain distorted guitars, palm-muted riffs, huge power-chords, harmonized
twin-lead guitars, shred guitar solos, whammy-bar dives, pinch harmonics, tapped

Psychedelic Rock
Psychedelic Rock is late-’60s rock designed to evoke altered states: fuzz/feedback
guitars, swirling studio effects, and extended, improvisational jams that pull from
blues, folk, jazz, and Indian raga (sitar, drones). Emerging around 1966 in the
US/UK, it seeded acid rock, prog, and later revivals

Genre Tags:
Psychedelic Rock, psychedelia, acid rock, British psychedelia, heavy psych, raga
rock, space rock, psychedelic pop, psychedelic folk, freakbeat, proto-prog
psychedelia, jam psychedelia, neo-psychedelia, experimental rock psychedelia

Mood Tags:
Trippy, hallucinatory, surreal, kaleidoscopic, dreamy, hazy, hypnotic, euphoric,
ecstatic, spaced-out, cosmic, otherworldly, mystical, mind-bending, exploratory,
expansive, swirling, drifting, glowing, luminous, euphoric, disorienting, eerie,
introspective, feverish, floating, meditative, ritualistic, transcendent, transportive

Instrument Tags:
Fuzz guitar, wah-wah guitar, feedback swells, droning guitar motifs, tremolo guitar,
phaser, flanger, rotary speaker, stereo panning, tape echo, spring reverb, artificial
double-tracking, tape reversal, varispeed, tape loops, sound effects collage,
Hammond organ, Farfisa organ, Mellotron, analog synth pads, 12-string guitar
jangle, sitar, tambura drone, tabla, hand percussion, congas, tambourine, solos,

Vocal Tags:
Reverb-drenched vocals, slapback/echo vocal, double-tracked lead vocal, phased
vocal, dreamy male tenor, airy female vocal, chant vocals, mantra-like hooks,
spoken-word passages, call-and-response refrains, stacked harmonies, wide stereo
backing vocals, ad-libbed vocal improvisation, fragmented lyrical imagery

Blues
Blues is African American-rooted vocal music that grew in the U.S. South around the
turn of the 20th century, shaped by work songs, field hollers, and spirituals. Its
signature feel comes from blue notes, call-and-response phrasing, and common
forms like the 12-bar blues and shuffle grooves.

Genre Tags:
Blues, traditional blues, Delta blues, country blues, Piedmont blues, Memphis blues,
Chicago blues, electric blues, Texas blues, West Coast blues, jump blues, classic
female blues, gospel blues, soul blues, swamp blues, British blues, blues rock

Mood Tags:
Soulful, lonesome, mournful, gritty, raw, weary, cathartic, aching, bittersweet,
rueful, hard-luck, introspective, reflective, moody, smoky, late-night, haunted,
simmering, tense, defiant, swaggering, sly, playful, seductive, earthy, intimate,
heartfelt, resigned, restless, hopeful, triumphant, celebratory, slow-burn

Instrument Tags:
Electric guitar, acoustic fingerpicking guitar, bottleneck slide guitar, resonator
guitar, tube amp overdrive, tremolo guitar, blues turnaround licks, 12-bar blues
changes, shuffle groove, swung eighths, walking bassline, upright bass, electric
bass, live drum kit, rimshot snare, cymbal shuffle, harmonica, piano, blues piano
fills, Hammond organ, horn section riffs, call-and-response instrumental answers

Vocal Tags:
Gritty male baritone vocal, raspy male tenor vocal, soulful female vocal, blue-note
bends, melismatic phrasing, spoken asides, moans and cries, shout accents,
intimate close-mic delivery, call-and-response with guitar, improvisational ad-libs,
gospel-tinged delivery, loose behind-the-beat phrasing, stacked backing harmonies

Southern Rock
Southern Rock is a ’70s American style blending blues-rock jams and boogie
grooves with country twang, led by dual guitars, Hammond organ, and driving
rhythm sections, often tied to Southern identity and storytelling. Key acts include
the Allman Brothers Band, Lynyrd Skynyrd, ZZ Top, and the Marshall Tucker Band

Genre Tags:
Southern Rock, southern rock, classic southern rock, ’70s southern rock, southern
blues rock, southern boogie rock, blues rock, country rock, roots rock, jam-oriented
rock, guitar-hero rock, barroom rock, hard southern rock, American rock,

Mood Tags:
Gritty, roadworn, rebellious, defiant, proud, soulful, earthy, rootsy, bluesy,
barroom, dusty, warm, laid-back, driving, rollicking, raucous, anthemic, heartfelt,
nostalgic, tough, rugged, honest, raw, punchy, triumphant, freewheeling,
sentimental, restless, down-home, electric, hard-luck

Instrument Tags:
Dual lead guitars, harmonized twin-guitar lines, slide guitar, blues turnaround licks,
boogie riffs, open-chord riffs, southern-style pentatonic leads, extended guitar
jams, wah-wah accents, Hammond organ, piano fills, electric bass guitar, driving
backbeat drums, shuffle groove, swung eighths, big tom fills, crash-heavy choruses,
occasional harmonica, live room feel

Vocal Tags:
Raspy male baritone vocal, gritty male tenor, Southern drawl, soulful belted,
shout-sung accents, loose behind-the-beat phrasing, call-and-response with guitar,
harmonized choruses, gang “hey” shouts, preacher-like ad-libs, conversational
verses, smoky late-night tone, powerhouse female southern-rock vocal

Acoustic Rock
Acoustic Rock is rock songwriting and backbeat performed with mostly acoustic
instruments—especially strummed or fingerpicked acoustic guitar—often in
stripped-down “unplugged” arrangements that spotlight melody, lyrics, and vocal
harmonies. It spans unplugged takes on hard rock to folk-leaning

Genre Tags:
Acoustic Rock, Unplugged Rock, Acoustic Alternative Rock, Acoustic Pop Rock,
Acoustic Hard Rock, Acoustic Classic Rock, Acoustic Singer-Songwriter Rock,
Acoustic Folk Rock, Adult Alternative Acoustic, Radio Acoustic Rock, Stripped-Down
Rock, Storyteller Rock, Campfire Rock, Semi-Acoustic Rock, Power-Ballad Acoustic

Mood Tags:
Intimate, Warm, Heartfelt, Reflective, Nostalgic, Tender, Vulnerable, Confessional,
Sincere, Calm, Soothing, Relaxed, Hopeful, Uplifting, Comforting, Romantic,
Melancholic, Soulful, Raw, Unfiltered, Cozy, Thoughtful, Sombre, Resilient, Dreamy,
Soft-Burn, Serene

Instrument Tags:
Steel-string acoustic guitar, 12-string acoustic guitar, fingerpicking, percussive
strumming, capo chords, acoustic guitar doubles, light electric lead accents,
acoustic bass guitar, piano, Rhodes electric piano, mandolin, cajón, brushed snare,
light drum kit, shakers, tambourine, handclaps,

Vocal Tags:
Close-mic intimate vocals, breathy male tenor, warm male baritone, clear female
alto, airy female soprano, singalong chorus delivery, stacked vocal harmonies, duet
harmonies, call-and-response backing lines, conversational verses, whispered lines,
emotional crack on peaks, ad-libbed melisma, low-reverb “in the room” vocal tone

Yacht Rock
Yacht Rock is ultra-polished West Coast soft rock from roughly 1975–84. Smooth
grooves, jazzy/R&B harmony, and breezy hooks built for FM radio. The nickname
was coined later by the 2005 comedy web series Yacht Rock, then adopted as a
retro genre label (Steely Dan, Toto, Michael McDonald, Christopher Cross)

Genre Tags:
Yacht rock, West Coast sound, adult-oriented rock, soft rock, smooth rock, soft pop
rock, blue-eyed soul, jazz-pop rock, R&B-influenced rock, adult contemporary rock,
late-’70s soft rock, early-’80s soft rock, studio soft rock, FM radio rock, smooth
soul-rock, polished pop-rock, coastal rock, LA soft rock, sophisticated pop rock

Mood Tags:
Smooth, breezy, laid-back, relaxed, warm, mellow, silky, chill, easygoing, romantic,
flirtatious, feel-good, optimistic, content, carefree, escapist, luxe, refined,
sophisticated, groovy, buoyant, light, dreamy, wistful, nostalgic, nighttime cruise,
mellow-funk, soft-glow, clean, comforting, tasteful, effortless

Instrument Tags:
Clean electric guitar, chorus guitar, muted funk guitar chops, smooth lead guitar
licks, electric piano, acoustic piano, synth pads, soft synth brass, tight drum kit,
rimshot snare, pocket basslines, melodic bass fills, smooth saxophone, horn stabs,
congas, shakers, tambourine, polished studio sheen,

Vocal Tags:
Smooth male tenor, warm male baritone, soul delivery, relaxed behind-the-beat
phrasing, clean bright lead vocal, soft raspy edge, falsetto touches, silky sustained
notes, close-mic intimate verses, tight backing harmonies, call-and-response
backing lines, lightly reverbed vocals, double-tracked lead vocal

Progressive Rock
Progressive Rock is late-’60s/’70s album-oriented rock built on extended forms, odd
meters, and virtuosic playing—often blending classical and jazz harmony with
studio experimentation and concept-driven storytelling. It’s strongly associated with
UK bands like Yes, Genesis, King Crimson, and Pink Floyd.

Genre Tags:
Progressive rock, prog rock, art rock, classic prog, British prog, progressive folk
rock, Canterbury scene, Italian progressive rock, krautrock-influenced, jazz-rock
fusion prog, eclectic prog, neo-progressive rock, progressive psychedelic rock

Mood Tags:
Epic, adventurous, ambitious, dramatic, theatrical, futuristic, mystical, otherworldly,
exploratory, expansive, intense, suspenseful, ominous, triumphant, awe-struck,
dreamy, surreal, hypnotic, transcendent, introspective, philosophical, restless,
virtuosic, complex, atmospheric, eccentric, surreal, moody, awe-inspiring, intricate

Instrument Tags:
Extended song structures, odd time signatures, complex drum patterns, live drum
kit, tom-heavy fills, melodic bass runs, bass pedals, distorted electric guitar leads,
sustained guitar solos, harmonized guitar, clean arpeggiated guitars, 12-string
guitar, wah guitar, fuzz guitar, Hammond organ, Mellotron, Moog synth, synth pads,
acoustic piano, electric piano, flute, orchestral textures, tape effects,

Vocal Tags:
Theatrical male tenor, warm male baritone, operatic delivery, dramatic phrasing,
long melodic lines, wide dynamic range, choral backing vocals,narrative/character
vocals, spoken-word passages, echo-drenched vocals, multi-tracked lead vocal,
expressive vibrato, chant-like refrains

Math Rock
Math Rock is guitar-led indie/alternative rock built on odd meters, stop-start
rhythms, and interlocking precise parts that feel “calculated.” Emerging in the late
’80s/early ’90s, it favors clean tones, tapping, and counterpoint, often instrumental
or with vocals treated like another instrument (Slint, Don Caballero, Battles, TTNG)

Genre Tags:
Math rock, indie math rock, instrumental math rock, Midwest math rock,
post-hardcore math rock, post-rock-adjacent math rock, twinkly math rock,
technical indie rock, experimental rock, Japanese math rock

Mood Tags:
Angular, intricate, cerebral, twitchy, kinetic, off-kilter, tense, playful, restless, bright,
punchy, tight, precise, wiry, jittery, bouncy, quirky, intense, dynamic, disciplined,
driving, airy, controlled, eruptive, focused, brainy, unpredictable, snappy, sharp

Instrument Tags:
Clean electric guitar, tapping riffs, two-guitar counterpoint, interlocking guitar lines,
staccato chord stabs, odd-meter riff cycles, stop-start hits, palm-muted math
chords, delay pedal shimmer, chorus pedal clean tone, loop pedals, syncopated
basslines, melodic bass runs, complex drum patterns, ghost-note snare, tight hi-hat
subdivisions, tom-driven fills, tempo shifts, unison break hits, dry close-mic drums

Vocal Tags:
Sparse vocals, vocals low in mix, male indie tenor, nasal male tenor, dry close-mic
vocal, talk-sung delivery, yelpy emo-adjacent vocal, monotone/detached vocal, tight
rhythmic phrasing, shouted accents, call-and-response shouts, chant refrains,
stacked indie harmonies, wordless “ooh” layers, instrumental-only tracks

shimmer, two-guitar counterpoint, syncopated bass runs, tight hi-hat subdivisions,
ghost-note snare, male indie tenor, dry close-mic, song about overthinking a text, stop-start
bridge, final chorus double

Shoegaze
Shoegaze is late-’80s/early-’90s UK alt-rock built from heavily processed guitars
(reverb/delay/chorus/fuzz) stacked into a dense “wall of sound,” with vocals mixed
low and dreamy. The name came from bands staring down at pedalboards onstage
(My Bloody Valentine, Slowdive, Ride)

Genre Tags:
Shoegaze, shoegazing, indie shoegaze, British shoegaze, Thames Valley shoegaze,
dream pop, ethereal wave, neo-psychedelia shoegaze, spacey shoegaze, heavy
shoegaze, nu-gaze, neo-shoegaze, blackgaze, grungegaze, post-rock shoegaze

Mood Tags:
Hazy, dreamy, hypnotic, immersive, blissed-out, melancholic, wistful, nostalgic,
romantic, yearning, introspective, moody, nocturnal, floating, airy, shimmering,
soft-focus, washed-out, surreal, cathartic, anxious, tense, bittersweet, euphoric,
glacial, warm, droning, luminous, brooding, distant, overwhelming, tranquil, woozy

Instrument Tags:
Wall-of-sound guitars, multiple layered guitars, reverb-heavy guitar, delay-soaked
guitar, chorus pedal shimmer, tremolo guitar, phaser guitar, flanger wash, reverse
reverb, fuzz distortion, overdrive, feedback swells, glide guitar tremolo-arm bends,
clean arpeggiated guitars, droning basslines, simple driving drum grooves,
ride-cymbal wash, tambourine, synth pad, noise textures, long sustain

Vocal Tags:
Buried lead vocals, distant-sounding vocals, reverb-drenched vocals,
double-tracked lead vocal, breathy male tenor, airy female alto, soft raspy edge,
monotone/detached delivery, wordless “ooh” layers, chant-like refrains, minimal
lyrical clarity, wide stereo backing vocals, gentle ad-libbed lines

Stoner Rock
Stoner Rock is slow-to-mid-tempo, riff-first heavy rock with down-tuned fuzz
guitars, thick bass, and hazy psychedelic/acid-rock textures, often with a retro ’70s
tone and extended jams. It crystallized in the early ’90s, strongly tied to California’s
Palm Desert/desert-rock scene (Kyuss) and doom-leaning peers like Sleep.

Genre Tags:
Stoner rock, stoner metal, stoner doom, desert rock, heavy psych, acid rock revival,
doom-psych, fuzz rock, riff rock, groove rock, psychedelic doom, heavy blues rock,
retro hard rock, sludge-adjacent stoner, Palm Desert sound, desert stoner rock

Mood Tags:
Hazy, hypnotic, woozy, trance-like, laid-back, swaggering, gritty, dusty, sunbaked,
heavy, crushing, thick, gritty-funky, head-nod, slow-burn, menacing, ominous,
nocturnal, smoky, narcotic, spaced-out, hallucinatory, hypnotic, cathartic, defiant,
rebellious, primal, thunderous, molten, relentless, groovy, raw, grimy, euphoric

Instrument Tags:
Down-tuned electric guitars, fuzz distortion, blown-out overdrive, tube amp
saturation, wah-wah leads, phaser/flanger wash, feedback swells, sustain-heavy
power riffs, one-riff vamps, extended jam sections, bass-forward mix, distorted

Industrial Rock
Industrial Rock fuses distorted rock guitars with industrial music’s mechanical
beats, samples, and synth textures—cold, abrasive, and often dance-floor tight.
Born from late-’70s/’80s post-punk/industrial scenes, it hit mainstream rock in the
’90s via Nine Inch Nails and Ministry.

Genre Tags:
Industrial Rock, Industrial Alternative, Industrial Punk, Post-Industrial Rock,
Electronic Rock, Industrial Metal, Industrial Hard Rock, Industrial Noise Rock, Synth
Rock, Electro-Industrial Rock, Industrial Groove Rock, Cyber Rock,

Mood Tags:
Aggressive, Abrasive, Mechanical, Dystopian, Cold, Claustrophobic, Relentless,
Tense, Angry, Menacing, Gritty, Confrontational, Dark, Cynical, Rebellious, Robotic,
Frenetic, Hypnotic, Pounding, Bleak, Nihilistic, Metallic, Violent, Threatening, Jagged,
Sardonic, Apocalyptic, Wired, Unhinged, Steely, Sinister, Punishing, Industrial,

Instrument Tags:
Distorted electric guitars, Down-tuned riffs, Palm-muted chugs, Feedback swells,
Noise, Sampled percussion, Clanging metal hits, sound effects, Drum machines,
Programmed drum loops, Sequenced synth bass, Synth stabs, Analog synth leads,
Filter sweeps, Gated reverb snare, Tight kick patterns, Loop-based grooves, Glitch,
lo-fi grit layers, FX-heavy production, Stutter edits, Sidechain pumping,

Vocal Tags:
Snarled male vocal, Harsh delivery, Aggressive talk-sung, Whisper-to-scream
dynamics, Distorted vocals, telephone vocal effect, Cold detached vocal tone,
Rhythmic chant refrains, Melodic hooky choruses, Layered backing vocals,
Pitch-shifted ad-libs, Spoken-word interludes, Screamed backing lines

Surf Rock
Surf Rock is early-’60s Southern California rock built on twangy electric guitar
drenched in “wet” spring reverb, rapid tremolo picking, and wave-like melodies over
driving backbeats. Popularized by Dick Dale and The Ventures, it also overlaps with
harmony-led surf-pop and later revivals.

Genre Tags:
Surf Rock, surf music, instrumental surf, surf guitar, surf instrumental rock, surf
pop, hot rod rock, California sound, reverb rock, classic surf, surf revival, modern
surf rock, garage surf, psychedelic surf, surf punk, indie surf

Mood Tags:
Sun-soaked, carefree, breezy, energetic, exhilarating, playful, upbeat, bright,
adventurous, youthful, beach-party, summer-night, cool, swaggering, kinetic,
driving, splashy, triumphant, rebellious, carefree, nostalgic, fun, rowdy, feel-good,
cinematic, coastal, windswept, punchy, vibrant, carefree-cruising

Instrument Tags:
Twangy electric guitar, spring reverb drip, outboard reverb unit tone, tremolo
picking, rapid alternate picking, whammy bar dips, tremolo effect, echo slap,
staccato riffs, glissando slides, minor-key surf melodies, double-stop leads, clean
amp bite, bass guitar walk, driving drum kit, rolling tom fills, snare backbeat,
ride-cymbal wash, crash accents, tambourine, saxophone leads, handclaps,

Vocal Tags:
Mostly instrumental, low-mix vocal, bright male tenor lead, surf-pop male harmony
stack, doo-wop backing vocals, tight block harmonies, “ooh/ah” layers,
call-and-response chants, shout hooks, reverb-drenched vocals, double-tracked
lead vocal, gang “hey” shouts

50's Doo Wop
50’s Doo Wop is ’50s R&B/rock vocal-group music: a tenor lead riding tight harmony
stacks and rhythmic nonsense syllables (“doo-wop”), often with little
instrumentation. Lyrics lean into teen love, longing, and heartbreak—street-corner
to studio hits (The Platters, The Drifters, Frankie Lymon & the Teenagers)

Genre Tags:
50s doo-wop, doo-wop, doo wop, classic doo-wop, vocal group R&B, street-corner
harmony, group harmony, doo-wop ballad, uptempo doo-wop, pop doo-wop,
rock-and-roll vocal group, teen doo-wop, 12/8 doo-wop, I-vi-IV-V progression

Mood Tags:
Romantic, sweet, innocent, nostalgic, yearning, tender, heartfelt, dreamy, wistful,
lonely, heartbroken, hopeful, sincere, sentimental, swooning, charming, playful,
flirty, youthful, earnest, warm, cozy, danceable, celebratory, naive, dopey-in-love,
tearful, comforting, sunny, moonlit, melodramatic, pleading, joyful

Instrument Tags:
A cappella, minimal backing band, upright bass, electric bass, clean electric guitar,
muted rhythm guitar, piano comping, doo-wop turnaround licks, tenor sax riffs,
baritone sax accents, light drum kit, brushed snare, handclaps, finger snaps,
tambourine, 12/8 triplet groove, simple backbeat, reverb-tinted mono mix

Vocal Tags:
Tenor lead vocal, falsetto lead, bass “bom-bom” lines, tight 4-part harmony, block
harmonies, call-and-response, “ooh/ah” pads, nonsense-syllable hooks (doo-wop,
sha-la-la), spoken break/recitative, doo-wop tag ending, double-tracked lead, lead +
trio backing, melismatic slides, close-mic vintage vocal tone

“bom-bom” lines, doo-wop tag ending, song about waiting by the phone for one call

Goth Rock
Goth Rock is dark, theatrical post-punk built on processed chorus/flange guitars,
high melodic bass, and drum-machine pulse or tribal toms, with deep baritone
vocals and romantic, morbid lyrics. Key architects include Bauhaus, Siouxsie and
the Banshees, The Cure, and The Sisters of Mercy

Genre Tags:
Goth Rock, Gothic Rock, Goth, Gothic Post-Punk, Post-Punk Goth, Dark Alternative
Rock, Batcave, Positive Punk, UK Goth, Deathrock, Darkwave Rock, Gothic New
Wave, Gothic Rock Revival, Second-Wave Goth, 80s Goth Rock, Modern Goth Rock

Mood Tags:
Dark, Brooding, Nocturnal, Melancholic, Romantic, Ominous, Eerie, Haunted, Cold,
Detached, Dramatic, Theatrical, Macabre, Funereal, Shadowy, Sinister, Mysterious,
Hypnotic, Dirge-like, Tense, Paranoid, Cathartic, Yearning, Despairing, Sardonic,
Ethereal, Dread-filled, Gloomy, Elegiac, Ritualistic, Gothic, Cinematic

Instrument Tags:
Chorus guitar shimmer, Flanger guitar wash, Reverb-drenched guitars, Clean
arpeggiated guitars, Scything guitar patterns, Minor-key riffs, Melodic high-register
basslines, Bass as lead melody, Drum machine pulses, Tribal tom patterns,
Dirge-like backbeat, Gated reverb snare, Synth pad drones, Cold analog synths,
Sparse guitar lines, Feedback swells, Atmospheric FX layers

Vocal Tags:
Deep male baritone, Droning vocal tone, Detached delivery, Theatrical phrasing,
Dark croon, Whisper-to-belt dynamics, Spoken-word interludes, Chant refrains,
Reverb-drenched vocals, Double-tracked lead, Call-and-response backing, Ghostly
harmony stack, Airy female alto, Wailing sustained notes, Low-mix vocals

Metal
Metal is riff-driven rock turned heavier and louder, defined by distorted guitars,
powerful drums, and virtuosic playing. Emerging in the late 1960s–early 1970s, it
branched into many substyles—from classic/heavy metal to thrash, death, black,
doom, power, and prog—often pairing intensity with theatrical imagery

Genre Tags:
Metal, Heavy metal, Traditional heavy metal, Classic metal, NWOBHM, Speed metal,
Thrash metal, Death metal, Black metal, Doom metal, Power metal, Progressive
metal, Symphonic metal, Groove metal, Extreme metal, Melodic death metal,
Industrial metal, Nu metal, Metalcore, Deathcore, Djent

Mood Tags:
Aggressive, Intense, Powerful, Heavy, Dark, Menacing, Furious, Defiant, Epic,
Dramatic, Ominous, Apocalyptic, Brooding, Sinister, Gritty, Relentless, Adrenaline,
Savage, Brutal, Ferocious, Crushing, Antagonistic, Rebellious, Wild, Stormy,
Vengeful, Razor-sharp, Chaotic, Melancholic, Grim, Unhinged

Instrument Tags:
Distorted electric guitars, Down-tuned guitars, Power-chord riffs, Palm-muted
chugs, Galloping riffs, Tremolo picking, Pinch harmonics, Fast picking, guitar solos,
Harmonized leads, Wah guitar leads, Feedback swells, Bass guitar, Double-kick
drums, Blast beats, Tight snare, Cymbal wash, Breakdowns, Layered production,

Vocal Tags:
Clean male tenor, Clean male baritone, Operatic clean vocals, Gritty clean vocal,
High screams, Harsh shouted vocals, Fry scream, Growled vocals, Death growls,
Black-metal shrieks, Whisper-to-scream dynamics, Chant refrains, Gang shouts,
Layered vocal doubles, Call-and-response backing, Spoken interludes

way

Metalcore
Metalcore fuses hardcore punk aggression with heavy-metal riffing: down-tuned
chugs, double-kick drums, and pit-ready breakdowns, often offset by melodic
choruses. It formed in the late ’80s/’90s and surged in the 2000s, ranging from raw
metallic hardcore to melodic/progressive styles (Converge, Killswitch Engage).

Genre Tags:
Metalcore, metallic hardcore, melodic metalcore, progressive metalcore, risecore,
electronicore, nu metalcore, post-hardcore-influenced metalcore, modern
metalcore, old-school metalcore, extreme metalcore, crossover metalcore,

Mood Tags:
Aggressive, cathartic, urgent, intense, defiant, anthemic, uplifting, vengeful,
anguished, determined, gritty, ruthless, volatile, energetic, adrenaline, furious,
desperate, resilient, heroic, dark, brooding, explosive, confrontational, emotional,
triumphant, razor-sharp, chaotic, punishing, pit-ready, relentless, fiery, raw, tense

Instrument Tags:
Down-tuned guitars, palm-muted chugs, percussive riffing, syncopated stop-start
riffs, breakdown sections, half-time drops, tremolo-picked accents, harmonized
lead melodies, fast alternate picking, double-kick drumming, blast-beat bursts,
snare punch, china-cymbal hits, tight gated production, bass drops (optional),
octave lead lines, pick bass attack, gang-shout breaks, tempo shifts

Vocal Tags:
Screamed male vocals, fry scream, harsh shouted delivery, clean melodic chorus,
scream-to-clean switches, layered vocal doubles, stacked chorus harmonies, gang
shout accents, spoken pre-breakdown lines, gritty clean baritone, high clean tenor,
screamed backing lines, chant refrains, breathy clean intros then full scream drops

Thrash Metal
Thrash Metal is an early-’80s extreme metal style that fuses NWOBHM precision
with hardcore punk speed: fast, aggressive, palm-muted low riffs, rapid
double-kick/snare attack, and shredding solos, often paired with critical or sarcastic
lyrics. Key pioneers include Metallica, Slayer, Megadeth, and Lamb of God

Genre Tags:
Thrash metal, thrash, old school thrash, modern thrash, Bay Area thrash, Teutonic
thrash, crossover thrash, technical thrash, melodic thrash, speed/thrash, blackened
thrash, deathrash, progressive thrash, hardcore-leaning thrash, mosh thrash

Mood Tags:
Furious, aggressive, relentless, razor-sharp, chaotic, hostile, defiant, rebellious,
confrontational, high-adrenaline, manic, urgent, militant, paranoid, cynical,
sarcastic, anti-establishment, apocalyptic, tense, angry, venomous, violent, gritty,
savage, breakneck, explosive, raw, punishing, ruthless, intense, unhinged

Instrument Tags:
Fast palm-muted riffing, rapid downpicking, tight chugs, galloping riffs, tremolo
picking bursts, chromatic runs, double-kick, rapid snare backbeats, cymbal choke
hits, tight hi-hat patterns, shredding lead guitar solos, harmonized lead lines,
whammy divebombs, pinch harmonics, aggressive bass pick attack, bass runs
under riffs, sharp high-gain tone, fast unison breaks, moshpit grooves

Vocal Tags:
Harsh shouted vocals, snarled delivery, barked phrasing, gritty yelled hooks,
high-register screams, gang shouts, call-and-response chants, rapid-fire vocal
cadence, double-tracked lead, layered shout backing, spoken taunts, aggressive
ad-libs,
