Deathcore
Deathcore is extreme metal that merges death metal’s blast beats and technical
riffing with metalcore’s breakdowns and hardcore bounce. Born in the late
’90s/early 2000s in North America, it favors drop tunings, brutal grooves, and
guttural vocals; touchstones include Suicide Silence, Whitechapel, and Lorna Shore.

Genre Tags:
Deathcore, modern deathcore, melodic deathcore, technical deathcore, progressive
deathcore, slam deathcore, downtempo deathcore, beatdown deathcore,
blackened deathcore, symphonic deathcore, deathrash-influenced deathcore

Mood Tags:
Brutal, savage, feral, violent, menacing, sinister, oppressive, claustrophobic,
ruthless, relentless, punishing, catastrophic, bloodthirsty, hostile, vengeful,
wrathful, hateful, unhinged, chaotic, frantic, panicked, paranoid, grim, nihilistic,
bleak, crushing, pummeling, pit-ready, confrontational, sadistic, monstrous, tense,

Instrument Tags:
Down-tuned 7-string/8-string guitars, ultra-tight palm-muted chugs, tremolo-picked
riff bursts, chromatic death-metal riffing, dissonant chord stabs, pinch harmonics,
whammy squeals, slam groove sections, two-step parts, half-time breakdown
drops, blast beats, double-kick grids, snare cannon, cymbal chokes, bass drops/sub
drops, distorted bass grind, tight gated production, abrupt tempo shifts

Vocal Tags:
Guttural lows, deep growls, high screams, fry screams, pig squeals, tunnel-throat
textures, gurgled vocal tones, rapid barked phrasing, callout lines before
breakdowns, gang shout accents, layered scream stacks, double-tracked lead,
whisper-to-scream dynamics, screamed backing lines, chant refrains

Nu Metal
Nu Metal is mid-’90s alternative metal that fuses heavy riffs with hip-hop
conventions—rapped cadences, turntables/samples—favoring downtuned,
syncopated groove riffs over guitar solos. It broke big in the late ’90s/early ’00s
through Korn, Limp Bizkit, Linkin Park, Slipknot

Genre Tags:
Nu Metal, nü-metal, nu-metal, alternative metal, rap metal, rap rock, funk metal
influence, industrial metal influence, groove metal influence, post-grunge adjacent,
modern nu metal, nu metal revival, nu metalcore, rapcore adjacent

Mood Tags:
Nu Metal, nü-metal, nu-metal, alternative metal, rap metal, rap rock, funk metal
influence, industrial metal influence, groove metal influence, post-grunge adjacent,
modern nu metal, nu metal revival, nu metalcore, rapcore adjacent

Instrument Tags:
Down-tuned 7-string guitars, drop tuning, syncopated chug riffs, bouncy “pogo”
riffs, staccato stops, palm-muted groove hooks, minimal guitar solos, wah accents,
pinch harmonics, heavy bass thump, funk-influenced bass pops, tight kick/snare
groove, hip-hop drum feel, drum samples, DJ turntables, scratching, sampling,
electronic textures, industrial hits, stereo FX sweeps

Vocal Tags:
Rapped verses, rhythmic shout-singing, harsh shouted vocals, screamed accents,
fry scream bursts, occasional growls, melodic chorus singing, whisper-to-yell
dynamics, double-tracked lead, layered hook choruses, call-and-response, gang
shout accents, spoken ad-libs, aggressive cadence delivery

Doom Metal
Doom Metal is slow, weight-first heavy metal built on down-tuned, sustained riffs,
dragging drums, and a bleak sense of impending dread. Heavily indebted to Black
Sabbath’s early heaviness, it coalesced as a distinct style in the 1980s and later split
into epic, stoner, sludge, death/doom, and funeral strains

Genre Tags:
Doom metal, traditional doom, epic doom metal, stoner doom, sludge doom,
funeral doom, death-doom, gothic doom, psychedelic doom, drone doom, doom
rock, doom-heavy metal, classic doom, modern doom metal, doom revival

Mood Tags:
Bleak, crushing, ominous, despairing, mournful, dread-filled, oppressive, grim,
haunted, somber, dirge-like, hopeless, melancholic, funereal, ritualistic, apocalyptic,
suffocating, paranoid, heavy, cavernous, miserable, tense, sinister, foreboding,
sluggish, monumental, brooding, nightmarish, cathartic, bitter, nihilistic, weary,

Instrument Tags:
Down-tuned guitars, sustained power-chord riffs, slow riff cycles, fuzzed-out
guitars, thick overdrive, doom shuffle groove, palm-muted chugs, feedback swells,
wah lead bends, minor-key lead lines, droning pedal tones, bass fuzz rumble, bass
as wall-of-sound, slow tom-heavy drums, big snare hits, cymbal wash, ride-cymbal
churn, spacious reverb, tape saturation grit, organ drone, synth pad drones

Vocal Tags:
Clean male baritone, gritty clean vocal, operatic clean vocals, wailing sustained
notes, lamenting delivery, reverb-drenched vocals, chant refrains, low-mix vocals,
double-tracked lead, layered harmonies, harsh shouted accents, death growls,
guttural lows, whisper-to-belt dynamics, screamed backing lines

Groove Metal
Groove Metal is early-’90s post-thrash built on mid-tempo, syncopated, down-tuned
riffs that lock with the kick and snare for a head-nod groove, trading thrash speed
for heavier pocket and percussive stops. Popularized by Pantera, Machine Head,
Sepultura, it later fed nu metal and metalcore

Genre Tags:
Groove Metal, groove thrash, post-thrash, neo-thrash, power groove, groove-heavy
metal, mid-tempo thrash, hardcore-leaning metal, modern groove metal, southern
groove metal, industrial-leaning groove metal, thrash-groove crossover

Mood Tags:
Aggressive, menacing, gritty, hostile, defiant, relentless, confrontational, cathartic,
violent, hard-hitting, punchy, tense, angry, brutal, ruthless, heavy, head-nod, raw,
savage, vengeful, grim, militant, dirty, cynical, primal, volatile, hard-edged,

Instrument Tags:
Down-tuned guitars, syncopated chug riffs, palm-muted groove hooks, percussive
stop-start hits, open-string riffs, pinch harmonics, squeal harmonics, whammy
divebombs, bluesy lead bends, minimal melodic runs, thick bass grind, picked bass
attack, kick-snare lock groove, tight double-kick bursts, halftime drops, tom-groove
beats, cymbal chokes, china cymbal accents, tight gated production

Vocal Tags:
Harsh shouted vocals, raspy barked delivery, gritty clean vocal, shout-sung hooks,
growled accents, scream bursts, rhythmic phrasing, double-tracked lead,
call-and-response shouts, gang vocal hits, spoken taunts, low-register male
baritone, strained high yells

Progressive Metal
Progressive Metal (prog metal) fuses heavy metal’s power and distortion with
progressive rock’s long-form songwriting—odd meters, tempo/key shifts, virtuoso
solos, and multi-section arrangements (often concept-leaning). It emerged in the
late ’80s and broke wider in the ’90s via bands like Queensrÿche and Dream Theater

Genre Tags:
Progressive Metal (prog metal) fuses heavy metal’s power and distortion with
progressive rock’s long-form songwriting—odd meters, tempo/key shifts, virtuoso
solos, and multi-section arrangements (often concept-leaning). It emerged in the
late ’80s and broke wider in the ’90s via bands like Queensrÿche and Dream Theater

Mood Tags:
Progressive metal, prog metal, technical prog metal, melodic progressive metal,
modern prog metal, classic prog metal, progressive death metal, instrumental prog
metal, avant-prog metal, progressive metalcore, cinematic prog metal

Instrument Tags:
Odd time signatures, meter changes, polyrhythms, syncopated riffs, down-tuned
guitars, tight palm-muted chugs, clean arpeggiated guitar intros, extended guitar
solos, harmonized lead lines, tapping passages, complex drum patterns,
double-kick bursts, tom-heavy fills, melodic bass counterlines, bass as lead melody,
keyboards/synth pads, ambient interludes, tempo shifts, layered production,

Vocal Tags:
Clean male tenor, clean male baritone, soaring choruses, theatrical phrasing, wide
dynamic range, clean-to-harsh switches, harsh shouted vocals, fry scream accents,
growled backing lines, layered vocal doubles, stacked harmonies, call-and-response
backing, spoken-word interludes, airy female alto, operatic clean vocals

Symphonic Metal
Symphonic Metal blends heavy riffs and pounding drums with orchestral color,
strings, choirs, and cinematic keyboard arrangements. Featuring operatic or
classically trained vocals and epic fantasy themes. It took shape in the early ’90s
and broke wider in the late ’90s via bands like, Nightwish, and Within Temptation

Genre Tags:
Symphonic metal, symphonic heavy metal, symphonic power metal, symphonic
gothic metal, symphonic black metal, symphonic death metal, operatic metal, opera
metal, orchestral metal, cinematic metal, neoclassical metal influence, symphonic
prog metal, symphonic doom metal, female-fronted symphonic metal

Mood Tags:
Epic, dramatic, majestic, triumphant, heroic, soaring, emotional, romantic,
haunting, dark, ominous, mystical, otherworldly, cinematic, grand, intense,
cathartic, suspenseful, adventurous, regal, melancholic, uplifting, fiery, ominous,
lush, theatrical, powerful, stormy, ethereal, apocalyptic, enchanting, bittersweet

Instrument Tags:
Orchestral strings, choir, symphonic keyboard, pipe organ, orchestral hits,
cinematic synth pads, brass swells, timpani, double-kick drums, tom rolls, big snare
hits, cymbal wash, distorted rhythm guitars, down-tuned guitars, harmonized lead
guitars, neoclassical lead runs, orchestral-style crescendos, layered production,

Vocal Tags:
Operatic female soprano, classically trained vocals, soaring clean male tenor, clean
male baritone, choir backing vocals, stacked harmonies, “beauty and the beast”
duet, harsh growled backing vocals, chant refrains, wide dynamic range, theatrical
phrasing, reverb-drenched vocals, call-and-response hooks, layered vocal doubles

Boom Bap
Boom Bap is classic East Coast hip-hop built on a hard kick-and-snare (“boom” +
“bap”), chopped sample loops, and head-nod swing, dominant in the late ’80s–’90s.
Production leans on vinyl grit, MPC-style chopping, DJ scratches, and sparse
arrangements that leave room for bar-heavy rapping.

Genre Tags:
Boom bap, Golden age hip hop, East Coast hip hop, Hardcore hip hop, Jazz rap,
Sample-based hip hop, Underground hip hop, 90s hip hop, Boom bap revival,
Backpack rap, Classic hip hop, DJ-centric hip hop

Mood Tags:
Gritty, raw, head-nod, punchy, streetwise, nostalgic, confident, defiant, intense,
focused, witty, sly, playful, cool, laid-back, hard-edged, relentless, confrontational,
hungry, competitive, triumphant, rebellious, introspective, conscious, sarcastic,
dark, tense, energetic, bold, rugged, soulful, gritty-smooth

Instrument Tags:
Hard kick drum, snappy snare, boom bap drum loop, swung hi-hats, rimshot
accents, breakbeat chops, sampled jazz loops, soul samples, funk breaks, horn
stabs, Rhodes chords, piano loops, upright bass samples, vinyl crackle, tape
saturation, filtered sample flips, short stab hits, DJ turntables, scratching, beat
juggling cuts, spoken samples, minimal synth pads

Vocal Tags:
Rapped male vocals, rapped female vocals, bar-heavy flow, laid-back flow,
aggressive cadence, rapid-fire bars, double-time bursts, storytelling rap,
battle-ready delivery, conscious rap delivery, call-and-response hook, ad-libs,
spoken intro, chant hook, group shouts, tight pocket phrasing

East Coast Hip Hop
East Coast Hip Hop is New York–centered rap defined by boom-bap drums,
chopped sample loops, DJ technique, and lyric-first delivery—dense rhyme
schemes, punchlines, and storytelling. From late ’80s–’90s golden age to modern
underground revivals, it stays gritty, head-nod, and bar-heavy.

Genre Tags:
East Coast hip hop, New York hip hop, boom bap, golden age hip hop, hardcore hip
hop, underground hip hop, backpack rap, jazz rap, sample-based hip hop,
conscious hip hop, mafioso rap, 90s hip hop, boom bap revival, DJ-centric hip hop

Mood Tags:
Gritty, raw, head-nod, punchy, hard-edged, streetwise, hungry, confident, defiant,
intense, focused, relentless, confrontational, competitive, cynical, sarcastic, tense,
dark, moody, gritty-smooth, nostalgic, soulful, reflective, serious, witty, slick, cold,
determined, paranoid, triumphant, rebellious, righteous, sharp

Instrument Tags:
Hard kick drum, snappy snare, boom bap drum loop, swung hi-hats, rimshot
accents, breakbeat chops, MPC-style chopping, 12-bit grit, vinyl crackle, tape
saturation, filtered sample flips, chopped jazz loops, soul samples, funk breaks,
horn stabs, Rhodes chords, piano loops, upright bass samples, DJ turntables,
scratching, beat-juggle cuts, spoken samples, sparse pads

Vocal Tags:
Rapped male vocals, rapped female vocals, bar-heavy flow, intricate rhyme
schemes, punchline delivery, storytelling rap, aggressive cadence, laid-back pocket
flow, rapid-fire bars, double-time bursts, conscious rap delivery, call-and-response
hook, ad-libs, group shouts, spoken intro/outro, tight pocket phrasing

West Coast Hip Hop
West Coast Hip Hop is California-rooted rap shaped by funk and cruising
grooves—heavy bass, laid-back swing, and (in the ’90s) G-funk synth leads and
talkbox textures. It rose with N.W.A (Ice Cube, Eazy-E, Dr. Dre), then exploded via
Dr. Dre, Snoop Dogg, and 2Pac; Bay Area pillars include Too $hort and E-40.

Genre Tags:
West Coast hip hop, West Coast rap, California rap, Los Angeles rap, Compton rap,
Long Beach rap, Bay Area rap, gangsta rap, G-funk, funk rap, pimp rap, mobb
music, hyphy, West Coast underground, West Coast revival

Mood Tags:
Laid-back, sun-baked, funky, smooth, cool, confident, swaggering, bouncy,
bass-heavy, cruising, party-ready, hazy, slick, playful, rebellious, streetwise, gritty,
hard-edged, menacing, tense, paranoid, confrontational, aggressive, provocative,
cinematic, nocturnal, triumphant, defiant, motivational, melancholic, celebratory

Instrument Tags:
Funk basslines, deep sub bass, Moog-style synth bass, whiny lead synth, P-funk
synth leads, talkbox hook, wah guitar licks, live bass guitar, sampled funk chops,
sparse piano stabs, string pad layers, claps/snaps, 808 kick, snappy snare, swung
hi-hats, ride-bounce groove, DJ turntables, scratching, vocal sample stabs,
call-and-response chant breaks

Vocal Tags:
Laid-back drawl, smooth baritone rap, rapped verses, sing-rap delivery, melodic
hook chorus, chant refrains, gang shouts, shouted ad-libs, calm-menace cadence,
storytelling rap, boast-heavy flow, rhythmic pocket phrasing, double-time bursts,
stacked hook doubles, female rap vocals, talkbox vocal hook

Prompt #3051

funky guitar licks, deep sub bass, claps + snaps, swung hi-hats, smooth male baritone rap,
melodic chorus, song about late-night cruising to clear your head, 16s/8s, long outro

Trap
Trap is Atlanta-rooted Southern hip hop built on booming 808 sub-bass, rapid
hi-hat rolls, snare rolls, and dark minor-key synth loops, often felt in half-time. It
was defined in the 2000s by T.I., Young Jeezy, and Gucci Mane, with later waves led
by Future and Young Thug

Genre Tags:
Trap, hip hop trap, Southern trap, Atlanta trap, street trap, hardcore trap, melodic
trap, dark trap, pop trap, drill-influenced trap, trap revival, modern trap, club trap,
EDM trap, orchestral trap

Mood Tags:
Menacing, gritty, tense, aggressive, ruthless, paranoid, cold, icy, cocky, bouncy,
celebratory, reckless, flashy, hedonistic, hungry, relentless, confrontational, defiant,
triumphant, anxious, ominous, dark, hypnotic, seductive, brash, sinister, volatile,
streetwise, high-energy, moody, dramatic

Instrument Tags:
808 sub bass, 808 bass slides/glides, distorted 808, deep 808 kick, rapid hi-hat rolls,
triplet hi-hats, stutter hats, snare rolls, clap on 3, halftime bounce, rimshot accents,
sparse drum breaks, dark synth bells, pluck melodies, minor-key pads, string stabs,
piano motif loop, brass hits, vocal chops, reverse cymbals, risers/drops, FX sweeps,
siren accents, minimal melodic space, tight modern mix

Vocal Tags:
Rapped male vocals, rapped female vocals, triplet flow, double-time bursts,
mumbled flow, melodic rap hooks, autotuned chorus, sing-rap delivery, aggressive
cadence, calm-menace cadence, chant hook, call-and-response, ad-libs, shouted
accents, layered hook doubles, spoken intro/outro, punchline delivery

Memphis Trap
Memphis Trap is the modern Memphis rap sound framed as trap: trunk-rattling
808s (often cowbells), bouncy triplet hats, dark piano loops. It draws from Three 6
Mafia’s horror-tinged underground, and today is pushed by Tay Keith–powered hits
and artists like Yo Gotti, Young Dolph, Moneybagg Yo, and Key Glock

Genre Tags:
Memphis Trap, Memphis rap, Tennessee trap, Southern trap, street trap, dark trap,
crunk-influenced trap, horrorcore-influenced trap, Memphis bounce, modern
Memphis sound, 90s Memphis influence, 808 cowbell trap, Tay Keith sound

Mood Tags:
Grimy, menacing, rowdy, aggressive, tense, paranoid, cold, ruthless, blunt,
confrontational, streetwise, hungry, hard-nosed, nocturnal, ominous, gritty,
rebellious, cocky, swaggering, bouncy, relentless, hostile, raw, urgent, dark,
claustrophobic, triumphant, reckless, intimidating, high-energy

Instrument Tags:
808 sub bass, distorted 808, sliding 808 glides, 808 cowbell, cowbell rhythm loop,
rapid triplet hi-hats, stutter hat fills, snare rolls, clap on 3, rimshot accents, halftime
bounce, punchy kick, sharp snare, dry minimal mix, dark piano motif, minor-key bell
loop, eerie synth pad, brass stabs, string stabs, siren FX, vocal chant samples,
dropouts/stop-time hits

Vocal Tags:
Rapped male vocals, rapped female vocals, Memphis drawl delivery, blunt talky
cadence, triplet flow, clipped pocket phrasing, aggressive cadence, calm-menace
cadence, chant hook, call-and-response, heavy ad-libs, shouted accents, gang
shouts, spoken intro/outro, hook doubles, minimalist hooks.

Atlanta Trap
Atlanta Trap is the original trap blueprint: half-time Southern bounce, 808 bass,
triplet hi-hats, snare rolls, and dark synth or piano loops. It was defined in the
2000s by T.I., Young Jeezy, Gucci Mane and producers DJ Toomp, Shawty Redd,
Zaytoven, then expanded by Future, Young Thug, Migos

Genre Tags:
Atlanta Trap, Trap, Southern Trap, Street Trap, Gangsta Trap, Dark Trap, Hardcore
Trap, Modern Trap, 2010s Trap, Trap Revival, Club Trap, Orchestral Trap,
Drill-Influenced Trap, Minimal Trap

Mood Tags:
Gritty, menacing, ruthless, tense, paranoid, cold, icy, hard-edged, confrontational,
aggressive, brash, blunt, streetwise, hungry, relentless, hostile, intimidating,
nocturnal, ominous, claustrophobic, grim, reckless, cocky, swaggering, bouncy,
triumphant, defiant, focused, urgent, volatile, sinister, hypnotic

Instrument Tags:
808 sub bass, 808 slides/glides, distorted 808, punchy 808 kick, rapid triplet hi-hats,
stutter hat fills, snare rolls, clap on 3, rimshot accents, halftime bounce, sparse
drum breaks, dark piano loop, minor-key synth pads, synth bell/pluck motif,
orchestral strings/brass stabs, reverse cymbals, risers/drops, FX sweeps, vocal chop
stabs, stop-time drops, tight modern mix

Vocal Tags:
Rapped male vocals, rapped female vocals, Southern drawl delivery, triplet flow,
clipped pocket phrasing, punchline delivery, calm-menace cadence, aggressive
cadence, chant hook, call-and-response, heavy ad-libs, shouted accents, hook
doubles, talky rap tone, spoken intro/outro, minimal hooks

Chicago Drill
Chicago Drill is early-2010s South Side rap built on dark, sparse beats—sliding 808s,
rattling hats. Popularized by Chief Keef (with producers Young Chop and DJ Kenn),
plus King Louie, Lil Durk, and Lil Reese, it sparked global drill scenes

Genre Tags:
Chicago drill, Drill, Drill rap, Chiraq, South Side drill, Early 2010s drill, Gangsta rap,
Trap-influenced drill, Midwestern hip hop, Horrorcore-influenced rap, Minimalist
drill, Street drill, Drill revival

Mood Tags:
Menacing, grim, cold, raw, stark, hostile, confrontational, paranoid, tense, ruthless,
unflinching, ominous, claustrophobic, angry, defiant, hard-nosed, blunt, aggressive,
relentless, volatile, chaotic, cynical, bleak, urgent, intimidating, gritty, violent,
adrenaline, fearless, streetwise, nihilistic

Instrument Tags:
808 sub bass, sliding 808 glides, distorted 808, punchy kick, sharp snare, triplet
hi-hats, rapid hat rolls, stutter hat fills, snare rolls, rimshot accents, halftime
bounce, sparse minor-key melodies, dark piano stabs, bell loop motifs, eerie synth
pads, stop-time drops, siren FX, vocal chant samples, dry minimal mix, repetitive
hook loops

Vocal Tags:
Rapped male vocals, rapped female vocals, deadpan delivery, blunt talky cadence,
calm-menace cadence, aggressive cadence, clipped pocket phrasing, triplet flow,
monotone bars, taunting callouts, chant hook, call-and-response, heavy ad-libs,
shouted accents, gang shouts, hook doubles, minimal hooks

UK Drill
UK Drill is London street rap (Brixton, early 2010s) built on 138–144 BPM half-time
grooves, sliding 808s, minor-key stabs, and syncopated hats. Popularized by Headie
One, Digga D, Unknown T, OFB, Russ Millions, Tion Wayne.

Genre Tags:
UK drill, London drill, British drill, drill rap, road rap, grime-influenced drill, UK drill
revival, street drill, minimalist drill, trap-influenced drill, UK underground rap,
commercial UK drill.

Mood Tags:
Menacing, cold, stark, gritty, grim, hostile, confrontational, paranoid, tense,
ruthless, unflinching, ominous, claustrophobic, angry, defiant, hard-nosed, blunt,
aggressive, relentless, volatile, chaotic, cynical, bleak, urgent, intimidating,
streetwise, fearless, nihilistic, moody, nocturnal, suspenseful, edgy, militant, raw.

Instrument Tags:
Sliding 808 glides, deep sub bass, distorted 808, punchy kick, sharp snare, snare
rolls, clap on 3, rimshot accents, skittering hi-hat patterns, triplet hat rolls,
syncopated hats, tresillo-style hat rhythm, halftime bounce, sparse minor-key
melodies, dark piano stabs, bell loop motifs, eerie synth pads, low string stabs,
vocal sample chops, stop-time drops, siren FX, dry minimal mix.

Vocal Tags:
Rapped male vocals, rapped female vocals, London accent delivery, deadpan
delivery, blunt talky cadence, calm-menace cadence, aggressive cadence, clipped
pocket phrasing, triplet flow, monotone bars, taunting callouts, chant hook,
call-and-response, heavy ad-libs

Cloud Rap
Cloud Rap is internet-born hip-hop built on airy synth pads, reverb-washed
melodies, and dreamy lo-fi haze under laid-back rap delivery. It blew up through Lil
B and Main Attrakionz, Clams Casino’s atmospheric production for A$AP Rocky, and
later scenes around Yung Lean, Bladee, and SpaceGhostPurrp.

Genre Tags:
Cloud rap, cloud trap, ambient hip hop, lo-fi hip hop, internet rap, underground hip
hop, experimental hip hop, vapor rap, psychedelic rap, emo-leaning rap, alternative
hip hop

Mood Tags:
Dreamy, hazy, ethereal, floaty, spaced-out, surreal, hypnotic, woozy, nocturnal,
melancholic, nostalgic, introspective, detached, euphoric, romantic, icy, ghostly,
misty, lush, meditative, trancey, psychedelic, soft-aggressive, weightless, glitched,
futuristic, lonely, calm, spaced, murky, vibey

Instrument Tags:
Airy synth pads, reverb-drenched keys, washed-out melodies, detuned bells,
ambient drones, filtered arpeggios, lo-fi samples, tape hiss, vinyl crackle, soft 808
sub bass, minimal kick, snappy clap, reverb snare, rolling hi-hats, triplet hat rolls,
sparse percussion, dreamy lead synth, pitch-shifted vocal chops, delay throws,
chorus/phaser textures

Vocal Tags:
Rapped male vocals, rapped female vocals, laid-back flow, drawled delivery,
monotone bars, melodic rap hooks, autotuned chorus, sleepy cadence, whisper
rap, talky rap tone, heavy ad-libs, doubled vocals, call-and-response hook, minimal
hooks, distant/reverb vocals, slurred phrasing, sing-rap delivery

Lo-fi Hip Hop
Lo-fi Hip Hop (chillhop) is mellow instrumental hip hop: dusty boom-bap drums,
jazzy loops, vinyl/tape hiss, and warm chords for study. Shaped by beatmakers J
Dilla and Nujabes (plus Madlib/MF DOOM’s Madvillainy touchstone), it went global
via YouTube 24/7 streams (Lofi Girl/College Music) and labels like Chillhop.

Genre Tags:
Lo-fi hip hop, Lo-fi beats, Chillhop, Study beats, Instrumental hip hop, Downtempo
hip hop, Jazz hop, Jazzy hip hop, Lo-fi boom bap, Beat tape, Chill-out hip hop,
Ambient hip hop, Bedroom beats, Lo-fi instrumental

Mood Tags:
Calm, mellow, cozy, dreamy, hazy, nostalgic, warm, relaxed, serene, peaceful,
focused, studious, soothing, reflective, bittersweet, intimate, rainy-day, late-night,
sleepy, meditative, airy, gentle, soft, comforting, wistful, tranquil, zen, understated,
content, mellow-sad, hopeful, detached

Instrument Tags:
Dusty boom-bap drums, swung hi-hats, lazy drum swing, rimshot snare, brushed
snare, vinyl crackle, tape hiss, cassette wobble, lo-fi filtering, SP-404 texture,
chopped jazz samples, Rhodes chords, mellow piano loop, clean jazz guitar,
vibraphone, upright bass, warm sub bass, soft sidechain, ambient pads, field
recordings, rain ambience, reverb tails, delay throws, sampled dialogue snippets

Vocal Tags:
Instrumental, No vocals, Vocal chops, Pitched vocal samples, Distant/reverb vocal
one-shots, Spoken-word snippets, Anime/dialogue samples, Minimal rap ad-libs,
Low-volume male rap, Low-volume female rap

Emo Rap
Emo Rap is SoundCloud-era hip hop that fuses trap drums with emo/pop-punk
guitar or samples, washed-out melodies, and confessional lyrics about heartbreak,
anxiety, addiction, and numbness. It broke mainstream through Lil Peep,
XXXTentacion, Juice WRLD and Trippie Redd.

Genre Tags:
Emo rap, emo hip hop, emo trap, sad rap, SoundCloud rap, alternative hip hop, pop
punk rap, trap rock, melodic rap, punk-rap, cloud rap influence, lo-fi trap, guitar
trap, emo revival rap

Mood Tags:
Melancholic, heartbroken, vulnerable, confessional, raw, anguished, lonely,
anxious, numb, exhausted, bittersweet, cathartic, desperate, self-destructive,
regretful, hopeless, pleading, romantic, resentful, angry, detached, moody, dark,
nocturnal, introspective, dreamy, hazy, fragile, yearning, nostalgic, shame, guilt

Instrument Tags:
808 sub bass, 808 slides, distorted 808, half-time trap bounce, rapid hi-hat rolls,
triplet hats, snare rolls, clap on 3, rimshot accents, emo guitar riff, pop-punk power
chords, clean chorus guitar, palm-muted guitar loop, sampled guitar,
reverb-drenched pads, airy synth lead, minor-key piano motif, vocal chops, reverse
cymbals, tape hiss, lo-fi saturation

Vocal Tags:
Rapped male vocals, rapped female vocals, sing-rap delivery, autotuned hook,
strained melodic chorus, breathy delivery, cracked voice, whisper lines, talky
confessional cadence, monotone bars, triplet flow, double-tracked vocals, stacked
hook harmonies, shouted ad-libs, scream accents, hook-heavy structure

Memphis Phonk
Memphis Phonk is phonk closest to 90s Memphis rap: lo-fi beats built from
chopped Memphis acapellas, eerie soul/horror samples, and heavy 808/cowbell
drums. Its roots trace to DJ Spanish Fly, DJ Zirk, DJ Squeeky and Three 6 Mafia;

Genre Tags:
Memphis Phonk, OG Phonk, Rare Phonk, Memphis Revival, 90s Memphis Influence,
Underground Phonk, Lo-fi Phonk, Cassette Phonk, Chopped and Screwed Phonk,
Horrorcore-Influenced Phonk, Southern Phonk, Sample-Based Phonk, Cowbell
Phonk, Tape-Hiss Phonk

Mood Tags:
Dark, eerie, gritty, dusty, raw, sinister, ominous, haunted, paranoid, tense, ruthless,
cold, bleak, nocturnal, hypnotic, trippy, grimy, streetwise, aggressive, unsettling,
moody, foreboding, claustrophobic, brooding, nihilistic, confrontational, dangerous,
ritualistic, eerie-calm, intense

Instrument Tags:
Chopped Memphis rap acapellas, pitched-down vocals, chopped edits, 808 bass,
distorted 808, sliding 808 glides, TR-808 cowbell, punchy kick, snare snaps, clap on
3, triplet hi-hats, hat rolls, snare rolls, rimshot accents, eerie piano stabs, minor-key
bell loops, horror/soul sample chops, vinyl crackle, tape hiss, cassette wobble,
low-pass filtering, sampler grit, stop-time drops, siren FX, reverb tails

Vocal Tags:
Sampled Memphis rap vocals, chopped vocal loops, chopped-and-screwed hook
chops, rapped male vocals, rapped female vocals, gritty talky cadence, deadpan
delivery, aggressive cadence, chant hook, taunting callouts, heavy ad-libs, shouted
accents, gang shouts, whispered doubles, hook doubles, minimal hooks

Mumble Rap
Mumble Rap is a mid-2010s offshoot of trap where slurred enunciation and heavy
Auto-Tune ride 808 driven beats, triplet hats, and hooky ad-libs. The label (often
pejorative) is tied to Future, Young Thug, Migos, Lil Uzi Vert, Playboi Carti, Desiigner.

Genre Tags:
Mumble rap, SoundCloud rap, Melodic trap, Atlanta trap, Trap rap, Auto-Tune rap,
New wave rap, Internet rap, Party trap, Hook rap, Lean rap, Minimal trap

Mood Tags:
Woozy, hazy, cocky, brash, reckless, playful, hedonistic, flexing, carefree, chaotic,
hype, bouncy, nocturnal, mischievous, defiant, flashy, carefree-dangerous,
drugged-out, spaced-out, taunting, confident, wild, triumphant, unserious,
sticky-hook, turn-up, loud, jittery

Instrument Tags:
808 sub bass, 808 slides/glides, distorted 808, punchy kick, sharp clap, snare rolls,
rimshot accents, rapid triplet hi-hats, stutter hat fills, bass drops, sparse synth
plucks, bell loop motifs, dark piano stabs, airy pad wash, vocal chop stabs,
risers/drops, stop-time drops, dry modern mix, blown-out distortion

Vocal Tags:
Rapped male vocals, rapped female vocals, slurred delivery, loose enunciation,
heavy Auto-Tune, melodic sing-rap, triplet flow, clipped pocket phrasing, talky
cadence, ad-lib heavy, chant hook, repetitive catchphrases, hook doubles, shouted
accents, playful taunts, mumble-like phrasing, lazy flow

Alternative Hip Hop
Alternative Hip Hop (aka alternative rap) is hip hop that blurs genres with funk,
rock, jazz, soul, reggae. Often using unconventional structures and concepts. It was
popularized by Native Tongues (De La Soul, A Tribe Called Quest), plus crossover
acts like Arrested Development, The Fugees, Outkast, and Kanye West.

Genre Tags:
Alternative hip hop, Alternative rap, Experimental hip hop, Abstract hip hop, Art
rap, Left-field hip hop, Indie hip hop, Underground hip hop, Conscious hip hop, Jazz
rap, Sampledelia,

Mood Tags:
Off-kilter, quirky, introspective, cerebral, surreal, anxious, gritty, moody, detached,
deadpan, defiant, rebellious, paranoid, alienated, chaotic, playful, ironic, satirical,
ominous, nocturnal, hypnotic, tense, melancholic, dreamy, eerie, twitchy,
uncomfortable, cathartic, provocative, confrontational, introspective-dark,
hopeful-glimmers

Instrument Tags:
Chopped samples, collage sampling, vinyl crackle, dusty boom-bap drums, swung
drums, breakbeats, glitch percussion, stutter edits, distorted bass, synth bass,
Rhodes chords, jazz piano stabs, guitar loops, distorted guitar, ambient pads, field
recordings, tape wobble, granular textures, weird synth leads, odd meter accents

Vocal Tags:
Rapped male vocals, rapped female vocals, spoken word, deadpan delivery,
monotone flow, laid-back flow, rapid-fire flow, syncopated cadence, conversational
rap, sing-rap, half-sung hooks, call-and-response hook, shouted accents,
whisper-rap, stacked doubles, chopped vocal samples

Trip Hop
Trip Hop is a UK-birthed, early-1990s downtempo style blending hip-hop
breakbeats and sampling with dub, soul/jazz, and cinematic textures, often dark
and atmospheric. The term was coined by the British press and the sound was
popularized via Bristol by Massive Attack, Portishead, and Tricky.

Genre Tags:
Trip hop, Downtempo, Bristol sound, Electronica, Chillout, Breakbeat, Experimental
downtempo, Cinematic beats, Abstract electronic, Acid jazz crossover, Dub-inflected
electronica, Turntablism

Mood Tags:
Smoky, moody, melancholic, brooding, noir, haunted, hypnotic, narcotic, sedated,
dreamy, foggy, intimate, tense, paranoid, eerie, ominous, desolate, urban,
cinematic, spacious, cold, late-night, sultry, bittersweet, reflective, uneasy, cathartic,
detached, shadowy, slow-burn

Instrument Tags:
Dusty breakbeats, chopped drum loops, swung snares, deep bass, dub bass,
low-pass filter sweeps, vinyl crackle, tape saturation, Rhodes electric piano,
minor-key pads, string samples, film-score samples, turntable scratches, reverb
snare, spring reverb, delay throws, reverse cymbals, tremolo guitar, vibraphone,
detuned synths, ambient textures, field recordings, downpitched vocal samples

Vocal Tags:
Female vocals, male vocals, breathy lead, whispered vocals, detached delivery,
spoken word, sing-rap, moody croon, layered harmonies, distant reverb vocal,
chopped vocal hook, ad-lib whispers

G Funk
G-Funk (gangsta-funk) is early-1990s West Coast hip-hop built on 1970s
funk—especially Parliament-Funkadelic—plus slow grooves, deep bass, and whiny
synth leads. Popularized by Dr. Dre’s The Chronic and artists like Snoop Dogg,
Warren G, and Nate Dogg, it pairs laid-back flows with street narratives.

Genre Tags:
G-funk, Gangsta-funk, West Coast hip hop, West Coast rap, 90s West Coast, Funk
rap, P-funk rap, Cali rap, LA rap, Gangsta rap, Golden era West Coast

Mood Tags:
Laid-back, smooth, cool, confident, cocky, swaggering, funky, bouncy, cruising,
summertime, sunny, carefree, hedonistic, playful, flirtatious, seductive, party-ready,
streetwise, menace-under-smiles, relaxed, triumphant, bold, rowdy, nostalgic,
celebratory, lowrider, late-night, mischievous

Instrument Tags:
High-pitched synth lead, portamento synth whistle, analog monosynth, Moog-style
bass, deep sub bass, rolling funk bassline, talkbox lead, wah guitar, clean funk
guitar chops, Rhodes electric piano, clavinet riffs, P-funk samples, horn stabs,
handclaps, rimshot accents, snappy snare, punchy kick, shaker hats, tambourine,
cowbell hits, scratch accents, siren synth, low-pass filter sweeps, tape saturation

Vocal Tags:
Rapped male vocals, rapped female vocals, laid-back flow, West Coast drawl,
smooth baritone rap, melodic sing-rap hook, crooned hook, talkbox vocals, chant
hook, call-and-response hook, ad-lib callouts, hook doubles, gang chants, relaxed
pocket phrasing

Conscious Rap
Conscious Rap centers lyrics on social critique, politics, community uplift, and
personal responsibility—often prioritizing message over braggadocio. Emerging in
the 1980s alongside political rap, it was popularized by Public Enemy and KRS-One,
and later carried by Common, Mos Def, Talib Kweli, and Kendrick Lamar

Genre Tags:
Conscious hip hop, Conscious rap, Socially conscious hip hop, Afrocentric rap,
Underground hip hop, Jazz rap, Alternative hip hop, Golden era hip hop,

Mood Tags:
Uplifting, Empowering, Defiant, Urgent, Political, Thoughtful, Reflective,
Introspective, Righteous, Militant, Analytical, Critical, Unapologetic, Passionate,
Poetic, Candid, Serious, Somber, Hopeful, Inspirational, Motivational,
Community-proud, Protest-ready, Confrontational, Compassionate, Heartfelt,
Determined, Resolute, Direct, Heavy, Raw, Truth-telling, Activist

Instrument Tags:
Dusty boom-bap drums, Breakbeat loops, Sampled jazz chords, Soul sample chops,
Horn stabs, Upright bass samples, Rhodes electric piano, Minor-key piano stabs,
Vinyl crackle, Turntable scratches, Spoken-word snippets, Crowd/chant samples,
Call-to-action drops, Sparse 808 support, Live drum kit layers, Tape saturation

Vocal Tags:
Rapped male vocals, Rapped female vocals, Clear enunciation, Storytelling verses,
Conscious lyricism, Preachy cadence, Spoken word, Passionate delivery,
Call-and-response hook, Chant hook, Baritone rap, Rapid articulate flow,
Conversational rap, Emphatic ad-libs, Hook doubles

crackle, Rhodes minor chords, upright bass layer, turntable scratches, verse-hook-verse,

Pop Rap
Pop Rap blends hip-hop verses and beats with pop songcraft—big melodic hooks,
radio-friendly structure, and polished production—aimed at mainstream crossover.
It took shape in the late ’80s (Run-D.M.C., LL Cool J, Beastie Boys) and hit mass pop
charts in the early ’90s via MC Hammer, Vanilla Ice, Tone Loc, and Young MC

Genre Tags:
Pop rap, Pop-rap, Pop hip hop, Melodic rap, Melodic hip-hop, Crossover rap, Radio
rap, Mainstream rap, Commercial hip hop, Hook rap, Dance rap, Party rap,
Contemporary pop rap, Pop-trap

Mood Tags:
Catchy, bright, upbeat, bouncy, confident, playful, flirtatious, romantic, feel-good,
celebratory, carefree, optimistic, glossy, polished, radio-ready, anthemic, energetic,
danceable, fun, cheeky, humorous, motivational, nostalgic, sentimental,
bittersweet, swaggering, bubbly, sunny, late-night party, summer cruise, easygoing

Instrument Tags:
Drum machine, punchy kick, snappy clap, tight snare, crisp hi-hats, 808 sub bass,
bass guitar, pop synth stabs, synth plucks, bright arpeggios, piano chords, guitar
riffs, sampled hooks, vocal chop accents, layered choruses, risers and drops,
sidechained pads, clean mix, stereo wideners, FX sweeps

Vocal Tags:
Rapped male vocals, rapped female vocals, sing-rap, melodic chorus, catchy hook,
clean enunciation, rhythmic flow, pop phrasing, hook doubles, harmony stacks,
ad-lib callouts, talk-rap, chant hook, light Auto-Tune, vocoder, call-and-response
hook

Chillhop
Chillhop is mellow, jazzy instrumental hip hop: downtempo breakbeats, warm bass,
Rhodes/guitar loops, and lo-fi hiss for study/relax listening. It surged in the 2010s
through YouTube streams and labels like Chillhop Music, with roots in beatmakers
such as J Dilla, Nujabes, and Madlib

Genre Tags:
Chillhop, Chill hop, Lo-fi hip hop, Lo-fi beats, Jazzy hip hop, Jazz hop, Instrumental
hip hop, Downtempo hip hop, Study beats, Chill beats, Beat tape, Cafe beats,
Bedroom beats

Mood Tags:
Relaxed, mellow, cozy, warm, calm, focused, soothing, serene, tranquil, peaceful,
meditative, mindful, reflective, nostalgic, dreamy, airy, gentle, soft, smooth,
laid-back, head-nod, content, sleepy, late-night, rainy-day, sunlit, bittersweet,
comforting, mellow-groove, uncluttered

Instrument Tags:
Dusty breakbeats, swung drums, soft kick, brushed snare, rimshot accents, shaker
hats, warm sub bass, upright bass, bass guitar, Rhodes electric piano, mellow piano
chords, jazz guitar comping, guitar loops, vibraphone, sax chops, trumpet snippets,
soul/jazz samples, chopped loops, vinyl crackle, tape saturation, cassette wobble,
hiss bed, ambient pads, field recordings, reverb tails, delay throws

Vocal Tags:
No vocals, sparse vocal chops, pitched vocal snippets, spoken-word samples, movie
dialogue samples, radio chatter samples, light ad-libs, mellow male rap, laid-back
flow, whisper-lean delivery, hook doubles, distant reverb vocal texture

Contemporary R&B
Contemporary R&B is modern rhythm-and-blues that blends classic soul vocals with
pop polish and hip-hop-influenced drums. It emphasizes groove, lush harmony
stacks, and emotionally direct themes like love, desire, heartbreak, and self-worth
(Janet Jackson, Mary J. Blige, TLC, Aaliyah, Usher, Beyoncé, Frank Ocean, SZA).

Genre Tags:
Contemporary R&B, Modern R&B, Pop R&B, Hip-Hop Soul, New Jack Swing, Neo
Soul, Alternative R&B, Trap Soul, Future R&B, Progressive R&B, Urban
contemporary, Soul-pop

Mood Tags:
Smooth, sensual, intimate, romantic, flirtatious, sultry, late-night, moody, tender,
vulnerable, confessional, yearning, heartbreak, wistful, dreamy, glossy, polished,
warm, cool, confident, empowered, seductive, atmospheric, hypnotic, simmering,
reflective, luxe, slow-burn, upbeat-sweet, playful, sassy, emotional, euphoric

Instrument Tags:
808 bass, deep bass guitar, drum machine, crisp hi-hats, syncopated hats, swung
percussion, snappy clap, finger snaps, rimshot accents, lush synth pads, warm
analog keys, Rhodes electric piano, Wurlitzer, filtered synth bass, bell plucks, airy
plucks, guitar licks, muted guitar, chorus guitar, ambient textures, reverse swells,
reverb tails, delay throws, sidechain pad pump, stereo wideners, clean modern mix

Vocal Tags:
Male vocals, Female vocals, Smooth croon, Breathier lead, Falsetto, Melismatic
runs, R&B riffs, Belted hook, Soft head voice, Talk-sung phrasing, Rap-sung cadence,
Harmony stacks, Hook doubles, Call-and-response hook, Ad-lib runs, Whispered
ad-libs, Intimate close-mic, Airy harmonies, Emotional delivery

90s R&B
90s R&B fused dance-floor New Jack Swing and hip-hop soul grooves with lush
slow-jam/Quiet Storm balladry. Key shapers include Teddy Riley, Babyface, Jimmy
Jam & Terry Lewis, Timbaland; artists include Boyz II Men, Mary J. Blige, TLC, Aaliyah,
Brandy, Usher, Toni Braxton, D’Angelo.

Genre Tags:
90s R&B, 1990s R&B, Contemporary R&B, New Jack Swing, Hip-Hop Soul, Quiet

Gospel R&B
Gospel R&B (aka Christian R&B/Rhythm & Praise) pairs modern R&B grooves, drum
machines, 808 bass, lush keys with Black church gospel harmony, melisma, and
lyrics focused on faith, love, healing, and testimony. Popularized by Mary Mary,
Yolanda Adams, Kirk Franklin, J. Moss, BeBe & CeCe Winans.

Genre Tags:
Gospel R&B, Christian R&B, Rhythm & Praise, R&P, Gospel/R&B, Urban
contemporary gospel, Contemporary gospel, Inspirational R&B, Gospel pop, Gospel
soul, Worship R&B, Neo-soul gospel, Hip-hop soul gospel, Radio gospel R&B

Mood Tags:
Uplifting, hopeful, faith-filled, encouraging, joyful, triumphant, grateful, prayerful,
heartfelt, sincere, earnest, comforting, healing, restorative, reassuring, soothing,
warm, tender, intimate, soulful, emotional, confessional, vulnerable, resilient,
determined, empowered, bold, peaceful, serene, inspired, liberated, communal,

Instrument Tags:
Drum machine pocket, tight kick, snappy clap, crisp hi-hats, syncopated hats, swung
percussion, finger snaps, handclaps, tambourine, live drum kit layers, 808 sub bass,
warm synth bass, bass guitar, Rhodes electric piano, Wurlitzer, gospel piano chords,
Hammond organ, organ swells, lush synth pads, string pads, choir pads, guitar licks,
call-and-response crowd/choir hits, stereo wideners, clean modern mix

Vocal Tags:
Male vocals, female vocals, lead and choir, choir harmonies, harmony stacks, gospel
belt, melismatic runs, R&B riffs, falsetto lift, breathy lead, powerful lead,
call-and-response hook, ad-lib runs, spoken testimony lines, chant hook, emotional
delivery, intimate close-mic

New Jack Swing
New Jack Swing is late-’80s/early-’90s R&B-pop built on hip-hop-informed swingbeat
drum machines, punchy snares, synth stabs, and gospel-leaning vocals dance music
with street bite and radio style. Coined in 1987 (Village Voice), it’s synonymous with
Teddy Riley (Guy), plus Janet Jackson, Bobby Brown, Bell Biv DeVoe, Keith Sweat.

Genre Tags:
New Jack Swing, Swingbeat, Swingbeat R&B, New Jill Swing, Hip-Hop Soul (early),
Urban contemporary, 80s R&B, 90s R&B, R&B-pop crossover, Funk-pop R&B,
Dance-pop R&B, Contemporary R&B

Mood Tags:
Upbeat, energetic, bouncy, dancefloor, confident, cocky, flirtatious, sexy, playful,
cheeky, flashy, feel-good, party-ready, romantic, fun, youthful, slick, polished,
vibrant, bold, swaggering, celebratory, carefree, mischievous, charismatic, smooth,
hot, infectious, hooky, bright, punchy, animated, poppy, kinetic, clubby,

Instrument Tags:
Swingbeat drum machine groove, offbeat accented 16th-triplet swing, punchy kick,
snappy snare, clap stack, syncopated hi-hats, shaker/tambourine, finger snaps,
percussion fills, sampled drum hits, MPC/SP-style chops, funky synth bass,
slap-bass synth, synth brass stabs, synth stabs, DX-style keys, bright chord stabs,
organ, short vocal hit samples, scratch accents, stop-time punches, clean pop mix,

Vocal Tags:
Male vocals, female vocals, smooth croon, rhythmic sing-talk, rap-sung phrasing,
pop-ready hook, call-and-response hook, harmony stacks, hook doubles, ad-lib
runs, melismatic runs, gospel-influenced belt, falsetto lift, group chants, spoken
ad-libs, energetic lead delivery, crisp enunciation, playful flirt cadence

reverb, clap stack, syncopated hats, tambourine, funky synth bass, synth brass stabs,
DX-style chord hits, stop-time hook punches, verse-prehook-hook, male vocals, rhythmic
sing-talk, pop-ready hook, song about stealing a dance before last call, breakdown then
double-chorus

Soul
Soul is a gospel-charged evolution of R&B that emerged in late-1950s/early-1960s
Black America, marked by gritty grooves, horn-driven arrangements, and emotional
vocals. Popularized by Ray Charles, Sam Cooke, Aretha Franklin, and James Brown

Genre Tags:
Soul, Classic soul, Southern soul, Deep soul, Memphis soul, Stax soul, Atlantic soul,
Detroit soul, Philly soul, Smooth soul, Soul-pop, Funk soul, Psychedelic soul, Soul
jazz, Soul blues, Gospel soul, Northern soul, Blue-eyed soul, Retro-soul

Mood Tags:
Passionate, heartfelt, raw, gritty, urgent, uplifting, triumphant, cathartic, yearning,
romantic, sensual, tender, vulnerable, confessional, pleading, bittersweet,
melancholic, hopeful, joyful, celebratory, fiery, preachy, sweaty, feel-good, warm,
nostalgic, sincere, earnest, dramatic, intimate, gospel-charged, ecstatic, resilient,
proud, soulful, smoky, late-night, slow-burn

Instrument Tags:
Tight backbeat, tambourine, handclaps, shaker, congas, bass guitar, walking bass,
upright bass, Hammond organ, Rhodes electric piano, Wurlitzer, gospel piano
chords, strings, string pads, horn section, trumpet, saxophone, trombone, electric
guitar rhythm chops, blues guitar licks, wah guitar, vibraphone, vinyl/tape
saturation, plate reverb, slapback echo, room ambience

Vocal Tags:
Male vocals, female vocals, belt, rasp, smooth croon, melismatic runs, soulful
shouts, lead and backing choir, harmony stacks, hook doubles, ad-lib runs,
improvised vocal riffs, sustained held notes, preacher-style exhortations, spoken
interjections, group chants, falsetto lift, emotional delivery, intimate close-mic

Motown Soul
Motown Soul is Detroit 1960s soul-pop engineered for crossover: strong backbeat
with tambourine, driving melodic electric bass, tight drums, punchy horns/strings,
and gospel harmonies. Built at Hitsville U.S.A. by the Funk Brothers; defined by The
Temptations, Marvin Gaye, Stevie Wonder, Smokey Robinson & the Miracles

Genre Tags:
Motown soul, Motown sound, Detroit soul, Classic soul, 60s soul, Soul-pop,
R&B-pop crossover, Pop soul, Northern soul (influence), Urban soul, Retro-soul,
Funk-influenced soul, Girl group soul

Mood Tags:
Bright, upbeat, danceable, feel-good, joyful, romantic, sweet, flirty, playful, youthful,
optimistic, buoyant, polished, slick, catchy, hooky, heartfelt, yearning, bittersweet,
triumphant, celebratory, lively, energetic, warm, nostalgic, charming, spirited,
motivational, communal, singalong, dramatic, tender, hopeful, classy, confident

Instrument Tags:
Tambourine backbeat, handclaps, tight snare crack, four-beat drum pattern,
syncopated hi-hats, shaker, finger snaps, melodic electric bassline, bass guitar fills,
piano chord stabs, vibraphone, glockenspiel, Hammond organ, clean rhythm guitar,
lead guitar licks, horn section, trumpet stabs, saxophone, trombone, orchestral
string, backing vox beds, bright AM-radio mix, tape saturation, plate reverb

Vocal Tags:
Male vocals, female vocals, lead + backing group, stacked harmonies, doo-wop
harmony stacks, call-and-response hook, choir-like backgrounds, tight unison lines,
sweet belt, gospel-leaning melisma, falsetto lift, energetic lead delivery, crisp
enunciation, hook doubles, ad-lib runs, group chants, dramatic held notes

Neo Soul
Neo Soul is a late-’80s/’90s soul revival blending 70s-style vocals/harmony with
jazz/funk chords and hip-hop-informed drums, favoring organic grooves and often
conscious themes. The term was coined by Kedar Massenburg in the late 1990s as
D’Angelo, Erykah Badu, Lauryn Hill, and Maxwell broke through.

Genre Tags:
Neo soul, Progressive soul, Soul revival, Alternative R&B, Hip-hop soul (influence),
Jazz soul, Funk soul (modern), Retro-soul, R&B fusion, Nu-soul, Organic R&B, Future
soul, Conscious soul, Neo-soul ballad, Groove soul

Mood Tags:
Warm, intimate, laid-back, mellow, smooth, sensual, romantic, tender, vulnerable,
confessional, introspective, reflective, thoughtful, poetic, soulful, dreamy, hypnotic,
late-night, smoky, nostalgic, soothing, meditative, earnest, sincere, confident, cool,
understated, raw, organic, groove-heavy, simmering, hopeful, -

Instrument Tags:
Fender Rhodes chords, Wurlitzer keys, jazz guitar, muted guitar, wah guitar, bass
guitar, deep funky bass, upright bass, rimshot snare, swung hi-hats, MPC drum
swing, breakbeat chops, tambourine, Hammond organ pads, synth pad, subtle horn
stabs, string swells, vibraphone, vinyl crackle texture, tape saturation, lo-fi hiss bed,
reverb tails, chord extensions (9ths/11ths/13ths)

Vocal Tags:
Male vocals, female vocals, smooth croon, breathy lead, intimate close-mic, falsetto
lift, melismatic runs, gospel-influenced, silky voice, conversational phrasing,
harmony stacks, choir-like backgrounds, ad-lib runs, spoken-word interludes

Trap Soul
Trap Soul is mid-2010s R&B built on trap percussion: 808 sub + hi-hat rolls over
moody minor-key pads/Rhodes, with melodic sing-rap confessionals about love,
temptation, and late-night regret. Bryson Tiller helped brand/popularize it; key
voices include PARTYNEXTDOOR and Brent Faiyaz.

Genre Tags:
Trap soul, Trap-soul, Trapsoul, Trap&B, TRap & B, Contemporary R&B, Alternative
R&B, Hip-hop soul, R&B/hip-hop crossover, Dark R&B, Emo R&B, Late-night R&B,
SoundCloud R&B, Melodic R&B rap, Minimal R&B, Atmospheric R&B,

Mood Tags:
Nocturnal, moody, brooding, intimate, confessional, melancholic, bittersweet,
seductive, simmering, tense, haunted, smoky, hazy, lonely, vulnerable, pleading,
jealous, possessive, regretful, apologetic, cold, detached, numb, paranoid, yearning,
obsessed, heartbroken, cocky, late-night-drive, rainy-window, underwater,
slow-burn, hypnotic, minimal, shadowy, intimate-close,

Instrument Tags:
808 sub bass, 808 slides/glides, distorted 808, halftime drum pocket, snare snaps,
rimshot, crisp hi-hats, hat rolls, clap layers, sparse kick patterns, filtered drum
breaks, minor-key synth pads, detuned pad, Rhodes electric piano, moody piano,
bell, dark synth, ambient textures, guitar swells, muted guitar, vocal chop stabs,
stereo wideners, sidechain pump, tape/vinyl noise texture, clean modern mix

Vocal Tags:
Male vocals, female vocals, sing-rap delivery, melodic rap, talk-sung cadence,
intimate close-mic, breathy lead, falsetto lift, Auto-Tune, harmony stacks, hook
doubles, ad-lib runs, melismatic runs, whispered ad-libs, emotional cracked tone,

Classic Country
Classic Country is mid-20th-century American country rooted in honky-tonk and
folk balladry, defined by twangy Telecaster, pedal steel, fiddle, shuffles and train
beats, and story-first lyrics about love, work, faith, and heartbreak. Popularized by
Hank Williams, Patsy Cline, George Jones, Merle Haggard.

Genre Tags:
Classic country, Traditional country, Old-time country, Honky-tonk, Nashville sound,
Bakersfield sound, Western swing, Country ballad, Country shuffle,

Mood Tags:
Heartbroken, lonesome, tender, sentimental, nostalgic, earnest, plainspoken,
bittersweet, warm, homespun, sincere, wistful, mournful, regretful, lonely-night,
barroom, rowdy, playful, cheeky, swaggering, defiant, jealous, reconciliatory,
hopeful, prayerful, small-town, blue-collar, road-weary, stoic, resilient, proud,

Instrument Tags:
Twangy Telecaster lead, chicken-pickin guitar, acoustic rhythm guitar, boom-chick
rhythm, pedal steel swells, lap steel, dobro, fiddle fills, mandolin chops, banjo rolls,
upright bass, walking bass, electric bass, honky-tonk piano, tack piano, Hammond
organ pads, brushed drums, train beat snare, shuffle groove, rimshot accents,
tambourine, handclaps, slapback echo, tape saturation,

Vocal Tags:
Male baritone lead, male tenor lead, female alto lead, female soprano lead, nasal
twang, cry-in-the-voice, controlled vibrato, straight-tone phrases, drawled delivery,
yodel inflections, talk-singing, spoken intro, duet vocals, close-harmony pairs, trio
harmonies, call-and-response hook, gospel backing vocals, stacked harmonies,
ad-lib tag lines, sustained belt chorus,

Outlaw Country
Outlaw Country is a 1970s rebel strain of country that rejected polished Nashville
production for raw honky-tonk grit, rock backbeat, and hard-lived
storytelling—freedom, vice, prison, and pride. Key figures include Willie Nelson,
Waylon Jennings, Johnny Cash, Kris Kristofferson, David Allan Coe.

Genre Tags:
Outlaw country, Progressive country, Honky-tonk, Hard country, Country rock,
Americana, Red dirt roots, Bakersfield-influenced, Western outlaw ballad,
Truck-driving country, Folk-country, Blues-country, Barroom country,

Mood Tags:
Rebellious, defiant, outlaw, rough, gritty, hard-luck, whiskey-soaked, smoky,
nocturnal, restless, dangerous, cocky, swaggering, unapologetic, blunt, cynical,
bitter, regretful, haunted, lonesome, lonely-night, angry, tense, resigned, stoic,
tough-love, cathartic, reckless, free-spirited, wild-hearted, fearless,

Instrument Tags:
Acoustic rhythm guitar, gritty electric rhythm guitar, twang Telecaster lead,
overdriven guitar solo, pedal steel swells, raw steel stabs, fiddle fills, harmonica
riffs, dobro slides, honky-tonk piano, tack piano, Hammond organ pads, barroom
shuffle drums, rock backbeat, train beat snare, rimshot accents, upright bass,
electric bass, room mics, tape saturation, slapback echo, live band feel,

Vocal Tags:
Male baritone lead, male tenor lead, raspy, gravelly delivery, drawled phrasing,
talk-singing, half-spoken verses, shouted accents, controlled vibrato, straight-tone
grit, bluesy bends, behind-the-beat phrasing, duet vocals, rough harmony stacks,
gang chorus hook, ad-lib, loose conversational cadence, close-mic intimacy,

Pop Country
Pop Country blends country storytelling and signature textures (acoustic guitar,
steel, fiddle) with pop’s hook-first songwriting and polished, chart-minded
production. It grew from Nashville’s push to reach wider audiences and has been
carried by crossover stars like Glen Campbell, Dolly Parton, and Shania Twain

Genre Tags:
Pop country, Country pop, Contemporary country, Mainstream country, Crossover
country, Radio country,

Mood Tags:
Bright, upbeat, feel-good, romantic, flirty, confident, empowering, anthemic,
euphoric, carefree, sunny, playful, sassy, charming, tender, heartfelt, sincere, warm,
nostalgic, hopeful, resilient, triumphant, dreamy, polished, sentimental, party,
yearning, loyal, fearless, bold, high-energy, soft-glow, bittersweet-happy,

Instrument Tags:
Acoustic guitar strum, palm-muted acoustic, bright clean electric guitar hooks,
chorus-guitar shimmer, country lead licks, pedal steel hook lines, steel swells, banjo
loop, mandolin, fiddle accents, pop piano chords, piano arpeggios, string pads,
synth pads, synth plucks, synth bass, sub bass, 808 drops, modern drum kit,
programmed drums, kick-snare pop backbeat, big chorus drums, clap stacks,
snaps, tambourine, shaker

Country Rock
Country Rock blends rock’s amplified backbeat and pop melody with country twang,
storytelling, and instruments like pedal steel, fiddle, mandolin, and Dobro. It
emerged in late 1960s–’70s Los Angeles/California via the Byrds and Gram Parsons,
and hit the mainstream with the Eagles and Linda Ronstadt.

Genre Tags:
Country rock, Country-rock, Country rock revival, California country rock, Country
rock ballad, Country rock anthem, Roots rock, Heartland rock, Americana rock,

Mood Tags:
Dusty, sunbaked, road-worn, free-spirited, rebellious, easygoing, laid-back, breezy,,
nostalgic, lonesome, heartbroken, yearning, earnest, plainspoken, hopeful,
resilient, restless, wide-open, pastoral, back-porch, barroom, confident, anthemic,
singalong, triumphant, reflective, hazy, mellow, jangly, wild-hearted,

Instrument Tags:
Acoustic rhythm guitar strum, 12-string acoustic, jangly electric guitar, clean electric
rhythm guitar, crunchy rock rhythm guitar, Telecaster twang lead, slide guitar, pedal
steel lead, pedal steel swells, Dobro slides, mandolin chops, fiddle fills, harmonica
riffs, piano chords, honky-tonk piano accents, Hammond organ, bass guitar, upright
bass, straight rock groove, shuffle groove, tambourine, shaker, handclaps, room
mics, tape saturation,

Vocal Tags:
Male lead vocals, female lead vocals, male tenor lead, male baritone lead, light
twang, belt, nasal edge, drawled phrasing, conversational delivery, gritty rasp,
close-harmony duos, stacked three-part harmonies, singalong chorus, sustained
high harmony lines, intimate close-mic verses, doubled lead vocals, shout accents

Americana
Americana (aka American roots music) is a modern umbrella for roots-based
songwriting that blends country, folk, blues, soul, bluegrass, gospel, and
rock—often acoustic-led, sometimes with a full electric band. The label crystallized
in the 1990s around artists like Lucinda Williams, Wilco, and Gillian Welch.

Genre Tags:
Americana, American roots music, Roots music, Roots rock, Americana rock,
Americana folk, Alternative country, Folk-country, Heartland rock, Country soul,

Mood Tags:
Earthy, rootsy, raw, unvarnished, intimate, heartfelt, sincere, reflective, nostalgic,
bittersweet, lonesome, weary, restless, searching, hopeful, resilient, defiant, gritty,
soulful, hymn-like, prayerful, humble, plainspoken, warm, stormy, haunted, tender,
cathartic, steady, grounded, autumnal, small-town, storytelling, honest,

Instrument Tags:
Fingerpicked acoustic guitar, flatpicked acoustic guitar, strummed acoustic rhythm,
12-string acoustic, resonator guitar, dobro slides, slide guitar, pedal steel swells, lap
steel, Telecaster twang fills, mandolin chops, banjo rolls, clawhammer banjo, fiddle
leads, harmonica riffs, upright bass, electric bass, stomp box, brushed drums, dry
snare backbeat, shuffle groove, tambourine, shaker, hand percussion, handclaps,
piano chords, Wurlitzer keys, Hammond organ pads, accordion accents, room mics,

Vocal Tags:
Male baritone lead, male tenor lead, female alto lead, female soprano lead, light
twang, gritty rasp, smoky tone, soulful belt, breathy close-mic, conversational,
storytelling phrasing, vibrato, harmony duos, stacked three-part harmonies, gospel
backing vocals, unison chorus, shouts , falsetto accents, whispered pickup lines,

Bluegrass
Bluegrass is high-energy acoustic string-band music born in the 1940s from Bill
Monroe’s Blue Grass Boys, built on banjo, mandolin, fiddle, guitar, and upright bass,
rapid solo “breaks,” and tight high-lonesome harmony singing.

Genre Tags:
Bluegrass, Traditional bluegrass, Classic bluegrass, Mountain bluegrass,
Appalachian bluegrass, Bluegrass gospel, Progressive bluegrass, Newgrass

Mood Tags:
High-energy, driving, rapid, urgent, fiery, exhilarating, punchy, tight, precise,
propulsive, lively, playful, rowdy, jubilant, celebratory, bright, sunny, wholesome,
homespun, backwoods, earthy, rootsy, earnest, heartfelt, sincere, nostalgic,
yearning, mournful, sorrowful, prayerful, devotional, hopeful, resilient, steadfast,

Instrument Tags:
5-string banjo, Scruggs-style banjo rolls, banjo vamp chops, banjo kickoff lick,
mandolin chop on the backbeat, mandolin tremolo, fiddle lead, fiddle double-stops,
twin-fiddle harmony, flatpicked acoustic guitar lead, crosspicking guitar patterns,
boom-chick acoustic rhythm, G-run walkdown, upright bass, slap upright bass,
walking bass runs, dobro/resonator slides, rapid break trading, unison riff kickoff,
stop-time hits, mic’d circle jam feel,

Vocal Tags:
High lonesome tenor lead, male tenor lead, male baritone harmony, female lead
vocals, close-harmony duet, tight trio stack, lead-in-the-middle harmony stack, low
baritone anchor, gospel quartet harmony, call-and-response chorus, sustained high
harmony line, twang, fast syllabic phrasing, conversational verse delivery, a cappella
intro tag, stacked chorus harmonies,

Honky Tonk
Honky Tonk is a dancehall country that rose in the 1940s, pairing fiddle and pedal
steel over a shuffle for loud bars and working-class crowds. Lyrics lean on
heartbreak, drinking and cheating. Figures include Ernest Tubb and Hank Williams

Genre Tags:
Honky-tonk, Honky tonk country, Dancehall country, Barroom country, Hard
country, Texas honky-tonk, Classic honky-tonk, Two-step, Country shuffle,

Mood Tags:
Rowdy, barroom, smoky, gritty, defiant, jealous, scorned, heartbroken, lonesome,
regretful, bitter, resentful, resigned, weary, road-worn, reckless, sarcastic, cheeky,
playful, swaggering, cocky, confrontational, cathartic, noisy, dancefloor, neon-lit,
unfaithful, desperate, pleading, stubborn, proud, working-class,

Instrument Tags:
Twangy Telecaster lead, chicken-pickin riffs, overdriven electric rhythm guitar,
acoustic boom-chick rhythm, pedal steel swells, steel stabs, fiddle fills, twin-fiddle
harmony, honky-tonk piano, tack piano, upright bass, slap upright bass, electric
bass, shuffle drums, two-beat kick pattern, train-beat snare, rimshot accents,
tambourine, handclaps, slapback echo, room mics, tape saturation,

Vocal Tags:
Male baritone lead, male tenor lead, female alto lead, nasal twang, cry-in-the-voice,
gritty rasp, drawled phrasing, talk-singing lines, sharp consonants, bluesy bends,
behind-the-beat delivery, belted chorus, harmony duos, tight trio stack,
call-and-response hook, shouted tag lines, ad-lib endings, close-mic intimacy,
sustained final note,

Country Gospel
Country Gospel delivers Christian hymns and testimony-style storytelling using
country grooves. Notable performers include The Carter Family, Webb Pierce, and
The Oak Ridge Boys.

Country Folk
Country Folk (folk-country/country-folk) blends country twang and rural storytelling
with folk’s singer-songwriter intimacy—typically acoustic-led, mellow, and
lyric-forward, with simple grooves and tasteful fiddle/steel/dobro colors.

Genre Tags:
Country folk, Folk-country, Country-folk, Folk country singer-songwriter, Acoustic
country, Roots folk-country, Rural folk, Appalachian folk-country,

Mood Tags:
Intimate, earnest, heartfelt, sincere, plainspoken, reflective, introspective, wistful,
nostalgic, bittersweet, tender, soft, warm, homespun, earthy, rootsy, pastoral, calm,
peaceful, gentle, quiet, thoughtful, vulnerable, honest, restrained, hopeful,
comforting, steady, grounded, lonesome, lonely-night, yearning, melancholic,
regretful, healing, humble, autumnal, sunrise, rainy-day, sentimental, cathartic,

Instrument Tags:
Fingerpicked acoustic guitar, flatpicked acoustic guitar, strummed acoustic rhythm,
12-string acoustic, mandolin, mandolin, fiddle fills, fiddle harmony lines, pedal steel
swells, lap steel pads, dobro/resonator slides, harmonica riffs, upright bass,
acoustic bass feel, light electric guitar swells, Wurlitzer piano, warm piano chords,
brushed drums, light kick-snare, shaker, tambourine, hand percussion, room mics,
tape saturation, natural reverb, minimal production,

Vocal Tags:
Male baritone lead, male tenor lead, female alto lead, female soprano lead, light
twang, folk-leaning delivery, close-mic intimacy, crisp diction, conversational,
narrative cadence, vibrato, soft belted chorus, harmony duet, tight trio harmonies

House
House is electronic dance music born in early-1980s Chicago clubs, driven by a
repetitive four-on-the-floor kick (typically ~115–130 BPM), drum machines and
synths, and looped disco/soul grooves. Tracks often feature sampled or sung
vocals, with claps, off-beat hats, and breakdown builds for the dancefloor.

Genre Tags:
House, Bass House, Deep House, Vocal House, Tropical House, Tech House,
Progressive House, French House, Electro House

Mood Tags:
Euphoric, uplifting, soulful, warm, ecstatic, celebratory, sexy, flirty, dreamy,
hypnotic, euphoric-peak, sunlit, afterhours, nocturnal, deep, smooth, lush, intimate,
romantic, confident, swaggering, funky, groovy, bouncy, playful, carefree, radiant,
blissful, driving, propulsive, energetic, focused, tense-build, cathartic, cosmic,
sweaty-club, glamorous, gritty-underground, nostalgic, hopeful, euphoric-release,

Instrument Tags:
Four-on-the-floor kick, 909 kick, 808 kick, 909 clap, offbeat closed hi-hat, open hat
on upbeats, shakers, tambourine, ride cymbal, congas, bongos, percussion fills, sub
bass, rolling bassline, synth bass, organ chords, piano stabs, Rhodes chords, synth
stabs, disco samples, string pads, airy pads, risers, white-noise sweeps, breakdown
drum fill, vocal chops, tape/vinyl texture,

Vocal Tags:
Diva vocals, soulful female lead, male lead vocals, stacked chorus harmonies, chant
hook, spoken word phrases, talk-sung lines, sampled vocal phrases, chopped vocal
loops, vocal chops, falsetto, rap verse feature, minimal vocal hook, sustained belted

90’s House
90’s House is 1990s club house built around 115–130 BPM four-on-the-floor, deep
basslines, big breakdowns, and piano/organ stabs, especially the Korg M1 “house
piano” and Organ 2 bass, plus soulful “diva” hooks across Chicago, NY garage, deep,
and Euro scenes.

Genre Tags:
90s house, Classic 90s house, 90s club house, 90s vocal house, Diva house

Mood Tags:
Euphoric, uplifting, soulful, warm, loved-up, sexy, flirty, confident, afterhours,
nocturnal, hypnotic, driving, propulsive, rolling, bouncy, funky, joyful, blissed-out,
nostalgic, bittersweet-euphoria, romantic, bold, victorious, playful, sassy, liberated,
throwback, feel-good, energized, trancey-lift, velvety, radiant,

Instrument Tags:
Four-on-the-floor kick, 909 kick, 909 clap, snare on 2&4, offbeat closed hi-hat, open
hat on upbeats, ride cymbal pattern, shaker loop, tambourine, conga/percussion
loops, tom fills, sub bass, rolling bassline, M1 Organ 2 bass, organ chords, M1 piano
stabs, rave piano chords, stab chords, string stabs, lush string pads, airy pads,
filtered disco samples, filter sweeps, pitch risers, white-noise sweeps, snare build
rolls, breakdown drum fills, impact hits, vocal chops, sampled hooks,

Vocal Tags:
Diva female lead vocals, soulful female lead, male house vocal, gospel runs, belted
chorus, call-and-response hook, chant hook, spoken hype lines, talk-sung verses,
sampled vocal phrases, chopped vocal loops, pitched vocal chops, stacked
harmonies, choir-style backing vocals, ad-lib runs, falsetto accents, reverb-delay
throws, stuttered vocal edits, a cappella breakdown moment, crowd-chant outro

Progressive House
Progressive House is a UK club style of house (118–132 BPM) four-on-the-floor,
rolling bass, gradual layer-by-layer arrangement, melodic stabs/pads, and big
breakdown-to-peak arcs. It was popularized in the ’90s by Sasha & John Digweed
and helped define the progressive club sound alongside early UK acts like Leftfield

Genre Tags:
Progressive house, 90s progressive house, UK progressive house, Progressive club
house, Underground progressive, Deep progressive

Mood Tags:
Hypnotic, rolling, deep, nocturnal, euphoric, uplifting, emotional, cinematic,
expansive, dreamy, moody, propulsive, driving, focused, immersive, floaty, soaring,
warm, mysterious, suspenseful, patient, gradual, afterhours, trancey-lift,
introspective, dramatic, atmospheric, relentless, smooth, futuristic, lush, pulsing

Instrument Tags:
Four-on-the-floor kick, tight punchy kick, snare/clap on 2&4, offbeat closed hi-hats,
open hat lifts, ride cymbal pattern, rolling bassline, sub bass, sidechain pump,
layered percussion loops, shaker loop, tom fills, chord stabs, detuned synth stabs,
warm pad stacks, airy pads, arpeggiated plucks, melodic synth lead, filter sweeps,
white-noise risers, snare build rolls, breakdown drum fills, impact hits,
reverb-drenched FX, delay throws, long breakdown, extended outro,

Vocal Tags:
Minimal vocal hook, sampled vocal phrases, chopped vocal loops, pitched vocal
chops, whispered spoken lines, short spoken hype line, airy female hook vocal,
male hook vocal, understated verses, trance-style vocal pads, call-and-response
hook, reverb-delay throws, stuttered vocal edits, a cappella breakdown
